title.raw: 卷二十六 天文志 Volume 26: Treatise on Astronomy

sentences.zh: 凡天文在圖籍昭昭可知者，經星常宿中外官凡百一十八名，積數七百八十三星，皆有州國官宮物類之象。

translations.literal: As for celestial patterns that can be clearly known from charts and records: fixed stars and regular lodges, inner and outer offices, number 118 names in all, totaling 783 stars, all corresponding to provinces, states, officials, palaces, and categories of things.
translations.idiomatic: As for celestial patterns that can be clearly known from charts and records: fixed stars and regular lodges, inner and outer offices, number 118 names in all, totaling 783 stars, all corresponding to provinces, states, officials, palaces, and categories of things.

sentences.zh: 其伏見蚤晚，邪正存亡，虛實闊骥，及五星所行，合散犯守，陵歷鬥食，彗孛飛流，日月薄食，暈適背穴，抱珥鸶蜺，迅雷風祅，怪雲變氣：此皆陰陽之精，其本在地，而上發于天者也。

translations.literal: Their hidden and visible phases, early and late rising, obliquity and correctness, survival and extinction, fullness and emptiness, broad and narrow spacing; and the motions of the Five Planets - conjunction, dispersion, encroachment, stationing, overstepping, crossing, eclipse; comets and broom stars, meteoric streams; sun and moon occultations; halos, crowns, backs, cavities, embraces, ear-like rays, rainbows; thunderbolts, demon-winds, strange clouds, and changing vapors - all are essences of yin and yang, rooted on earth and manifesting above in heaven.
translations.idiomatic: Their hidden and visible phases, early and late rising, obliquity and correctness, survival and extinction, fullness and emptiness, broad and narrow spacing; and the motions of the Five Planets - conjunction, dispersion, encroachment, stationing, overstepping, crossing, eclipse; comets and broom stars, meteoric streams; sun and moon occultations; halos, crowns, backs, cavities, embraces, ear-like rays, rainbows; thunderbolts, demon-winds, strange clouds, and changing vapors - all are essences of yin and yang, rooted on earth and manifesting above in heaven.

sentences.zh: 政失於此，則變見於彼，猶景之象形，鄉之應聲。

translations.literal: When governance fails here, anomalies appear there, like a shadow reflecting form or an echo answering sound.
translations.idiomatic: When governance fails here, anomalies appear there, like a shadow reflecting form or an echo answering sound.

sentences.zh: 是以明君睹之而寤，飭身正事，思其咎謝，則禍除而福至，自然之符也。

translations.literal: Therefore enlightened rulers, seeing these, awaken, discipline themselves and correct affairs, reflect on faults and make amends; then disaster is removed and blessing arrives - this is nature's own sign.
translations.idiomatic: Therefore enlightened rulers, seeing these, awaken, discipline themselves and correct affairs, reflect on faults and make amends; then disaster is removed and blessing arrives - this is nature's own sign.

sentences.zh: 中宮天極星，其一明者，泰一之常居也，旁三星三公，或曰子屬。

translations.literal: In the central palace, the Celestial Pole star: its one bright star is Taiyi's constant abode; nearby three stars are the Three Excellencies, or, some say, royal sons.
translations.idiomatic: In the central palace, the Celestial Pole star: its one bright star is Taiyi's constant abode; nearby three stars are the Three Excellencies, or, some say, royal sons.

sentences.zh: 後句四星，末大星正妃，餘三星後官之屬也。

translations.literal: Behind, in a four-star hook, the terminal great star is the principal consort; the other three belong to consorts and harem offices.
translations.idiomatic: Behind, in a four-star hook, the terminal great star is the principal consort; the other three belong to consorts and harem offices.

translations.literal: Around them are twelve guarding stars, the frontier ministers.
translations.idiomatic: Around them are twelve guarding stars, the frontier ministers.

translations.literal: All are called the Purple Palace.
translations.idiomatic: All are called the Purple Palace.

sentences.zh: 前列直斗口三星，隨北耑銳，若見若不見，曰陰德，或曰天一。

translations.literal: Before it, lined straight at the Dipper mouth, are three stars tapering northward, sometimes seen, sometimes unseen, called Yin Virtue, or, some say, Heavenly One.
translations.idiomatic: Before it, lined straight at the Dipper mouth, are three stars tapering northward, sometimes seen, sometimes unseen, called Yin Virtue, or, some say, Heavenly One.

sentences.zh: 紫宮左三星曰天槍，右四星曰天棓。

translations.literal: Three stars to the left of the Purple Palace are called Heavenly Spear; four to the right are called Heavenly Baton.
translations.idiomatic: Three stars to the left of the Purple Palace are called Heavenly Spear; four to the right are called Heavenly Baton.

sentences.zh: 後十七星絕漢抵營室，曰閣道。

translations.literal: Behind them, seventeen stars crossing the Milky River to Ying chamber are called Pavilion Road.
translations.idiomatic: Behind them, seventeen stars crossing the Milky River to Ying chamber are called Pavilion Road.

sentences.zh: 北斗七星，所謂「旋、璣、玉衡以齊七政」。

translations.literal: The Northern Dipper's seven stars are what is meant by "the rotating pivot, armillary, and jade balance by which the seven regulations are aligned."
translations.idiomatic: The Northern Dipper's seven stars are what is meant by "the rotating pivot, armillary, and jade balance by which the seven regulations are aligned."

sentences.zh: 杓攜龍角，衡殷南斗，魁枕參首。

translations.literal: Its handle draws the Dragon Horn; its balance meets the Southern Dipper; its bowl rests by Orion's head.
translations.idiomatic: Its handle draws the Dragon Horn; its balance meets the Southern Dipper; its bowl rests by Orion's head.

translations.literal: What is established at dusk is the handle.
translations.idiomatic: What is established at dusk is the handle.

translations.literal: The handle points to southwest from Hua westward.
translations.idiomatic: The handle points to southwest from Hua westward.

translations.literal: What is established at midnight is the balance.
translations.idiomatic: What is established at midnight is the balance.

translations.literal: The balance points to the central plains between the Yellow and Ji rivers.
translations.idiomatic: The balance points to the central plains between the Yellow and Ji rivers.

translations.literal: What is established at dawn is the bowl.
translations.idiomatic: What is established at dawn is the bowl.

translations.literal: The bowl points to the northeast beyond sea and Tai.
translations.idiomatic: The bowl points to the northeast beyond sea and Tai.

sentences.zh: 斗為帝車，運于中央，臨制四海。

translations.literal: The Dipper is the emperor's chariot, revolving at the center and overlooking and commanding the four seas.
translations.idiomatic: The Dipper is the emperor's chariot, revolving at the center and overlooking and commanding the four seas.

sentences.zh: 分陰陽，建四時，均五行，移節度，定緒紀，皆繫於斗。

translations.literal: Dividing yin and yang, establishing four seasons, balancing five phases, shifting seasonal measures, and fixing chronology - all are tied to the Dipper.
translations.idiomatic: Dividing yin and yang, establishing four seasons, balancing five phases, shifting seasonal measures, and fixing chronology - all are tied to the Dipper.

sentences.zh: 斗魁戴筐六星，曰文昌宮：一曰上將，二曰次將，三曰貴相，四曰司命，五曰司祿，六曰司災。

translations.literal: On the Dipper bowl, six stars like a basket are called the Wenchang palace: first Upper General, second Next General, third Noble Chancellor, fourth Controller of Fate, fifth Controller of Emoluments, sixth Controller of Calamities.
translations.idiomatic: On the Dipper bowl, six stars like a basket are called the Wenchang palace: first Upper General, second Next General, third Noble Chancellor, fourth Controller of Fate, fifth Controller of Emoluments, sixth Controller of Calamities.

translations.literal: Within the bowl is the enclosure of nobles.
translations.idiomatic: Within the bowl is the enclosure of nobles.

sentences.zh: 魁下六星兩兩而比者，曰三能。

translations.literal: Below the bowl, six stars paired two by two are called Three Capacities.
translations.idiomatic: Below the bowl, six stars paired two by two are called Three Capacities.

translations.literal: If the Three Capacities are uniform in color, ruler and ministers are harmonious.
translations.idiomatic: If the Three Capacities are uniform in color, ruler and ministers are harmonious.

translations.literal: If not uniform, there is estrangement and disorder.
translations.idiomatic: If not uniform, there is estrangement and disorder.

translations.literal: If the handle's auxiliary star is bright and near, supporting ministers are intimate and strong.
translations.idiomatic: If the handle's auxiliary star is bright and near, supporting ministers are intimate and strong.

translations.literal: If faint and small, they are distant and weak.
translations.idiomatic: If faint and small, they are distant and weak.

sentences.zh: 杓端有兩星：一內為矛，招搖；

translations.literal: At the handle tip are two stars: inner one is Spear, called Zhaoyao.
translations.idiomatic: At the handle tip are two stars: inner one is Spear, called Zhaoyao.

translations.literal: The outer one is Shield, called Heavenly Bee.
translations.idiomatic: The outer one is Shield, called Heavenly Bee.

sentences.zh: 有句圜十五星，屬杓，曰賤人之牢。

translations.literal: There is a hooked circle of fifteen stars attached to the handle, called the enclosure of lowly people.
translations.idiomatic: There is a hooked circle of fifteen stars attached to the handle, called the enclosure of lowly people.

sentences.zh: 牢中星實則囚多，虛則開出。

translations.literal: If stars in the enclosure are full, there are many prisoners; if sparse, prisons open and release.
translations.idiomatic: If stars in the enclosure are full, there are many prisoners; if sparse, prisons open and release.

sentences.zh: 天一、槍、棓、矛、盾動搖，角大，兵起。

translations.literal: If Heavenly One, Spear, Baton, Spear-tip, or Shield shake or swell at the horns, warfare rises.
translations.idiomatic: If Heavenly One, Spear, Baton, Spear-tip, or Shield shake or swell at the horns, warfare rises.

translations.literal: Eastern Palace Azure Dragon: Fang and Xin.
translations.idiomatic: Eastern Palace Azure Dragon: Fang and Xin.

sentences.zh: 心為明堂，大星天王，前後星子屬。

translations.literal: Xin is the Bright Hall; its great star is the Heavenly King, with front and rear stars as royal offspring.
translations.idiomatic: Xin is the Bright Hall; its great star is the Heavenly King, with front and rear stars as royal offspring.

translations.literal: It should not be aligned straight.
translations.idiomatic: It should not be aligned straight.

translations.literal: If straight, the king miscalculates.
translations.idiomatic: If straight, the king miscalculates.

translations.literal: Fang is Heavenly Treasury, also called Heavenly Team of Four.
translations.idiomatic: Fang is Heavenly Treasury, also called Heavenly Team of Four.

translations.literal: Its yin side is the right trace horse.
translations.idiomatic: Its yin side is the right trace horse.

translations.literal: Beside it are two stars called Collar.
translations.idiomatic: Beside it are two stars called Collar.

translations.literal: North of Collar one star is called Gui.
translations.idiomatic: North of Collar one star is called Gui.

translations.literal: To the northeast, a bent set of twelve stars is called Banner.
translations.idiomatic: To the northeast, a bent set of twelve stars is called Banner.

translations.literal: Within the Banner, four stars are Heavenly Market.
translations.idiomatic: Within the Banner, four stars are Heavenly Market.

sentences.zh: 天市中星眾者實，其中虛則耗。

translations.literal: If stars in Heavenly Market are numerous, it is full; if empty, there is depletion.
translations.idiomatic: If stars in Heavenly Market are numerous, it is full; if empty, there is depletion.

translations.literal: South of Fang are many stars called Cavalry Office.
translations.idiomatic: South of Fang are many stars called Cavalry Office.

translations.literal: Left Horn signifies governance.
translations.idiomatic: Left Horn signifies governance.

translations.literal: Right Horn signifies military command.
translations.idiomatic: Right Horn signifies military command.

translations.literal: The Great Horn is the Heavenly King's imperial court seat.
translations.idiomatic: The Great Horn is the Heavenly King's imperial court seat.

sentences.zh: 其兩旁各有三星，鼎足句之，曰攝提。

translations.literal: On both sides are three stars each, hooked like tripod legs, called Sheti.
translations.idiomatic: On both sides are three stars each, hooked like tripod legs, called Sheti.

sentences.zh: 攝提者，直斗杓所指，以建時節，故曰「

translations.literal: Sheti aligns with where the Dipper handle points to establish seasonal terms, therefore called
translations.idiomatic: Sheti aligns with where the Dipper handle points to establish seasonal terms, therefore called

translations.literal: Sheti-ge.
translations.idiomatic: Sheti-ge.

translations.literal: Kang is the ancestral temple and governs illness.
translations.idiomatic: Kang is the ancestral temple and governs illness.

translations.literal: Its two great stars to south and north are called Southern Gate.
translations.idiomatic: Its two great stars to south and north are called Southern Gate.

translations.literal: Di is Heavenly Root and governs epidemics.
translations.idiomatic: Di is Heavenly Root and governs epidemics.

translations.literal: Wei is the nine sons, called ruler and ministers.
translations.idiomatic: Wei is the nine sons, called ruler and ministers.

translations.literal: If severed or broken off, there is discord.
translations.idiomatic: If severed or broken off, there is discord.

sentences.zh: 箕為敖客，后妃之府，曰口舌。

translations.literal: Ji is the arrogant guest, treasury of empress and consorts, called mouth-and-tongue.
translations.idiomatic: Ji is the arrogant guest, treasury of empress and consorts, called mouth-and-tongue.

translations.literal: If Mars encroaches and stations at Horn, there are halberds, i.e. war.
translations.idiomatic: If Mars encroaches and stations at Horn, there are halberds, i.e. war.

translations.literal: Fang and Xin are ominous for rulers.
translations.idiomatic: Fang and Xin are ominous for rulers.

translations.literal: Southern Palace Vermilion Bird: Balance-beam and scale.
translations.idiomatic: Southern Palace Vermilion Bird: Balance-beam and scale.

translations.literal: Balance and Taiwei are the court of the Three Luminaries.
translations.idiomatic: Balance and Taiwei are the court of the Three Luminaries.

translations.literal: Twelve guarding stars form the court-fence: west are generals,
translations.idiomatic: Twelve guarding stars form the court-fence: west are generals,

translations.literal: east are chancellors,
translations.idiomatic: east are chancellors,

translations.literal: four southern stars are law enforcers,
translations.idiomatic: four southern stars are law enforcers,

translations.literal: center is Main Gate,
translations.idiomatic: center is Main Gate,

translations.literal: left and right are side gates.
translations.idiomatic: left and right are side gates.

translations.literal: Within side gates are six stars: the feudal lords.
translations.idiomatic: Within side gates are six stars: the feudal lords.

translations.literal: Inside them are five stars: seats of the Five Thearchs.
translations.idiomatic: Inside them are five stars: seats of the Five Thearchs.

sentences.zh: 後聚十五星，曰哀烏郎位；

translations.literal: Behind is a cluster of fifteen stars called Ailang positions;
translations.idiomatic: Behind is a cluster of fifteen stars called Ailang positions;

translations.literal: beside them one great star is the general's seat.
translations.idiomatic: beside them one great star is the general's seat.

sentences.zh: 月、五星順入，軌道，司其出，所守，天子所誅也。

translations.literal: If moon and five planets enter in proper order along the track, governing their emergence and station, it signifies those the Son of Heaven will punish.
translations.idiomatic: If moon and five planets enter in proper order along the track, governing their emergence and station, it signifies those the Son of Heaven will punish.

sentences.zh: 其逆入，若不軌道，以所犯名之；

translations.literal: If they enter against order or off the track, the omen is named by what they violate.
translations.idiomatic: If they enter against order or off the track, the omen is named by what they violate.

sentences.zh: 中坐，成形，皆群下不從謀也。

translations.literal: If they strike the central seats and form patterns, it means subordinates are not following plans.
translations.idiomatic: If they strike the central seats and form patterns, it means subordinates are not following plans.

translations.literal: This is especially severe with Venus and Mars.
translations.idiomatic: This is especially severe with Venus and Mars.

sentences.zh: 廷藩西有隨星四，名曰少微，士大夫。

translations.literal: West of the court-fence are four attendant stars called Shaowei, representing scholar-officials.
translations.idiomatic: West of the court-fence are four attendant stars called Shaowei, representing scholar-officials.

translations.literal: Balance belongs to Xuanyuan, body of the Yellow Dragon.
translations.idiomatic: Balance belongs to Xuanyuan, body of the Yellow Dragon.

translations.literal: Its front great star symbolizes the female ruler.
translations.idiomatic: Its front great star symbolizes the female ruler.

translations.literal: Nearby small stars are imperial attendants and harem affiliates.
translations.idiomatic: Nearby small stars are imperial attendants and harem affiliates.

translations.literal: Moon and five planets in guarding or encroaching are interpreted as with the Balance.
translations.idiomatic: Moon and five planets in guarding or encroaching are interpreted as with the Balance.

translations.literal: Eastern Well governs water affairs.
translations.idiomatic: Eastern Well governs water affairs.

sentences.zh: 火入之，一星居其左右，天子且以火為敗。

translations.literal: If Mars enters it and one star stays to either side, the Son of Heaven will soon be defeated by fire.
translations.idiomatic: If Mars enters it and one star stays to either side, the Son of Heaven will soon be defeated by fire.

translations.literal: The west-curving star of Eastern Well is called Axe.
translations.idiomatic: The west-curving star of Eastern Well is called Axe.

translations.literal: To the north is Northern River.
translations.idiomatic: To the north is Northern River.

translations.literal: To the south is Southern River.
translations.idiomatic: To the south is Southern River.

translations.literal: Between the two rivers and Heavenly Gate is passes and bridges.
translations.idiomatic: Between the two rivers and Heavenly Gate is passes and bridges.

translations.literal: Carriage Ghosts govern ghost-sacrifice matters.
translations.idiomatic: Carriage Ghosts govern ghost-sacrifice matters.

translations.literal: Its central white star is called Hostage.
translations.idiomatic: Its central white star is called Hostage.

sentences.zh: 火守南北河，兵起，穀不登。

translations.literal: If Mars stations at Southern or Northern River, war rises and grain fails.
translations.idiomatic: If Mars stations at Southern or Northern River, war rises and grain fails.

sentences.zh: 故德成衡，觀成潢，傷成戉，禍成井，誅成質。

translations.literal: Thus virtue is completed at Balance, observation at Moat, injury at Axe, disaster at Well, and punishment at Hostage.
translations.idiomatic: Thus virtue is completed at Balance, observation at Moat, injury at Axe, disaster at Well, and punishment at Hostage.

translations.literal: Liu is the bird's beak and governs trees and grasses.
translations.idiomatic: Liu is the bird's beak and governs trees and grasses.

translations.literal: Qixing is the neck, the circular palace, governing urgent affairs.
translations.idiomatic: Qixing is the neck, the circular palace, governing urgent affairs.

translations.literal: Zhang is the crop, functioning as kitchen, governing banquets and guests.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang is the crop, functioning as kitchen, governing banquets and guests.

translations.literal: Yi is the wings and governs distant guests.
translations.idiomatic: Yi is the wings and governs distant guests.

translations.literal: Zhen is the chariot and governs wind.
translations.idiomatic: Zhen is the chariot and governs wind.

sentences.zh: 其旁有一小星，曰長沙，星星不欲明；

translations.literal: Beside it is one small star called Changsha; this star should not be bright.
translations.idiomatic: Beside it is one small star called Changsha; this star should not be bright.

sentences.zh: 明與四星等，若五星入軫中，兵大起。

translations.literal: If bright equal to the four stars, or if five planets enter Zhen, great war rises.
translations.idiomatic: If bright equal to the four stars, or if five planets enter Zhen, great war rises.

sentences.zh: 軫南眾星曰天庫，庫有五車。

translations.literal: South of Zhen, many stars are Heavenly Storehouse, and in the storehouse are five chariots.
translations.idiomatic: South of Zhen, many stars are Heavenly Storehouse, and in the storehouse are five chariots.

sentences.zh: 車星角，若益眾，及不具，亡處車馬。

translations.literal: If the chariot stars are angular, or increase excessively, or are incomplete, there will be nowhere for chariots and horses.
translations.idiomatic: If the chariot stars are angular, or increase excessively, or are incomplete, there will be nowhere for chariots and horses.

translations.literal: Western Palace Xianchi is called Heavenly Five Moats.
translations.idiomatic: Western Palace Xianchi is called Heavenly Five Moats.

translations.literal: The Five Moats are carriage houses of the Five Thearchs.
translations.idiomatic: The Five Moats are carriage houses of the Five Thearchs.

translations.literal: If Mars enters, drought.
translations.idiomatic: If Mars enters, drought.

translations.literal: If Venus enters, war.
translations.idiomatic: If Venus enters, war.

translations.literal: If Mercury enters, flood.
translations.idiomatic: If Mercury enters, flood.

translations.literal: Within are three pillar stars.
translations.idiomatic: Within are three pillar stars.

translations.literal: If the pillars are incomplete, war rises.
translations.idiomatic: If the pillars are incomplete, war rises.

translations.literal: Kui is called Sealed Boar and signifies channels and ditches.
translations.idiomatic: Kui is called Sealed Boar and signifies channels and ditches.

translations.literal: Lou signifies gathering the masses.
translations.idiomatic: Lou signifies gathering the masses.

translations.literal: Wei is Heavenly Granary.
translations.idiomatic: Wei is Heavenly Granary.

translations.literal: Its many southern stars are called storehouses.
translations.idiomatic: Its many southern stars are called storehouses.

sentences.zh: 昴曰旄頭，胡星也，為白衣會。

translations.literal: Mao is called Banner-Head, a Hu star, and signifies assemblies in white clothing.
translations.idiomatic: Mao is called Banner-Head, a Hu star, and signifies assemblies in white clothing.

sentences.zh: 畢曰罕車，為邊兵，主弋獵。

translations.literal: Bi is called Hunting Chariot, signifying frontier troops and governing hunting and archery.
translations.idiomatic: Bi is called Hunting Chariot, signifying frontier troops and governing hunting and archery.

translations.literal: Small stars beside its great star are Attached Ears.
translations.idiomatic: Small stars beside its great star are Attached Ears.

sentences.zh: 附耳搖動，有讒亂臣在側。

translations.literal: If Attached Ears shake, slanderous and disorderly ministers are at the ruler's side.
translations.idiomatic: If Attached Ears shake, slanderous and disorderly ministers are at the ruler's side.

translations.literal: Between Mao and Bi is Heavenly Street.
translations.idiomatic: Between Mao and Bi is Heavenly Street.

translations.literal: Its shaded side is yin states.
translations.idiomatic: Its shaded side is yin states.

translations.literal: Its lit side is yang states.
translations.idiomatic: Its lit side is yang states.

translations.literal: Shen is the White Tiger.
translations.idiomatic: Shen is the White Tiger.

translations.literal: Its three straight stars are called Weighing Stone.
translations.idiomatic: Its three straight stars are called Weighing Stone.

sentences.zh: 下有三星，銳，曰罰，為斬艾事。

translations.literal: Below are three sharp stars called Punishment, governing beheading and execution.
translations.idiomatic: Below are three sharp stars called Punishment, governing beheading and execution.

translations.literal: Its outer four stars are left and right shoulders and thighs.
translations.idiomatic: Its outer four stars are left and right shoulders and thighs.

sentences.zh: 小三星隅置，曰觜觿，為虎首，主葆旅事。

translations.literal: A small set of three stars set at an angle is called Beak-and-Talon, the tiger's head, governing military encampment affairs.
translations.idiomatic: A small set of three stars set at an angle is called Beak-and-Talon, the tiger's head, governing military encampment affairs.

translations.literal: To its south are four stars called Heavenly Latrine.
translations.idiomatic: To its south are four stars called Heavenly Latrine.

translations.literal: Below Heavenly Latrine one star is called Heavenly Arrow.
translations.idiomatic: Below Heavenly Latrine one star is called Heavenly Arrow.

translations.literal: If Arrow is yellow, auspicious.
translations.idiomatic: If Arrow is yellow, auspicious.

translations.literal: If blue, white, or black, inauspicious.
translations.idiomatic: If blue, white, or black, inauspicious.

sentences.zh: 其西有句曲九星，三處羅列：一曰天旗，二曰天苑，三曰九斿。

translations.literal: To its west is a hooked curve of nine stars in three rows: first Heavenly Banner, second Heavenly Park, third Nine Streamers.
translations.idiomatic: To its west is a hooked curve of nine stars in three rows: first Heavenly Banner, second Heavenly Park, third Nine Streamers.

sentences.zh: 其東有大星曰狼，狼角變色，多盜賊。

translations.literal: To its east is a great star called Wolf; if Wolf's horns change color, there are many bandits.
translations.idiomatic: To its east is a great star called Wolf; if Wolf's horns change color, there are many bandits.

translations.literal: Below are four stars called Bow, aligned with Wolf.
translations.idiomatic: Below are four stars called Bow, aligned with Wolf.

sentences.zh: 比地有大星，曰南極老人。

translations.literal: Near the horizon is a great star called Southern Pole Elder.
translations.idiomatic: Near the horizon is a great star called Southern Pole Elder.

translations.literal: If Elder appears, governance is stable.
translations.idiomatic: If Elder appears, governance is stable.

translations.literal: If not, war rises.
translations.idiomatic: If not, war rises.

translations.literal: It is normally watched at southern suburb around autumnal equinox.
translations.idiomatic: It is normally watched at southern suburb around autumnal equinox.

translations.literal: Northern Palace Black Tortoise: Xu and Wei.
translations.idiomatic: Northern Palace Black Tortoise: Xu and Wei.

translations.literal: Wei is roof-covering house.
translations.idiomatic: Wei is roof-covering house.

translations.literal: Xu governs weeping and mourning affairs.
translations.idiomatic: Xu governs weeping and mourning affairs.

sentences.zh: 其南有眾星，曰羽林天軍。

translations.literal: To its south are many stars called Feathered Forest Heavenly Army.
translations.idiomatic: To its south are many stars called Feathered Forest Heavenly Army.

translations.literal: West of the army is Rampart, or, some say, Axe.
translations.idiomatic: West of the army is Rampart, or, some say, Axe.

translations.literal: Beside it one great star: Northern Fall.
translations.idiomatic: Beside it one great star: Northern Fall.

sentences.zh: 北落若微亡，軍星動角益稀，及五星犯北落，入軍，軍起。

translations.literal: If Northern Fall is faint or absent, and army stars shift, horn, or thin out, and if five planets encroach Northern Fall and enter Army, war rises.
translations.idiomatic: If Northern Fall is faint or absent, and army stars shift, horn, or thin out, and if five planets encroach Northern Fall and enter Army, war rises.

translations.literal: This is especially severe with Mars, Venus, and Mercury.
translations.idiomatic: This is especially severe with Mars, Venus, and Mercury.

translations.literal: If Mars enters, army has anxiety.
translations.idiomatic: If Mars enters, army has anxiety.

translations.literal: If Mercury enters, there is flood calamity.
translations.idiomatic: If Mercury enters, there is flood calamity.

translations.literal: If Jupiter or Saturn enters, army is favorable.
translations.idiomatic: If Jupiter or Saturn enters, army is favorable.

sentences.zh: 危東六星，兩兩而比，曰司寇。

translations.literal: East of Wei are six stars in pairs called Minister of Justice.
translations.idiomatic: East of Wei are six stars in pairs called Minister of Justice.

sentences.zh: 營室為清廟，曰離宮、閣道。

translations.literal: Yingshi is the clear temple, also called separated palace and pavilion road.
translations.idiomatic: Yingshi is the clear temple, also called separated palace and pavilion road.

translations.literal: In the Milky River, four stars are called Heavenly Team of Four.
translations.idiomatic: In the Milky River, four stars are called Heavenly Team of Four.

translations.literal: Beside them one star is called Wangliang.
translations.idiomatic: Beside them one star is called Wangliang.

translations.literal: When Wangliang drives the horses, chariots and cavalry fill the fields.
translations.idiomatic: When Wangliang drives the horses, chariots and cavalry fill the fields.

translations.literal: Beside are eight stars crossing the Milky River, called Heavenly Crossbar.
translations.idiomatic: Beside are eight stars crossing the Milky River, called Heavenly Crossbar.

translations.literal: Beside Heavenly Crossbar is River Star.
translations.idiomatic: Beside Heavenly Crossbar is River Star.

translations.literal: If River Star moves, people ford waters.
translations.idiomatic: If River Star moves, people ford waters.

translations.literal: Pestle and Mortar, four stars, are south of Wei.
translations.idiomatic: Pestle and Mortar, four stars, are south of Wei.

sentences.zh: 匏瓜，有青黑星守之，魚鹽貴。

translations.literal: Bottle Gourd: if guarded by blue-black star, fish and salt become expensive.
translations.idiomatic: Bottle Gourd: if guarded by blue-black star, fish and salt become expensive.

translations.literal: Southern Dipper is the temple; to its north is Jian star.
translations.idiomatic: Southern Dipper is the temple; to its north is Jian star.

translations.literal: Jian star is the banner.
translations.idiomatic: Jian star is the banner.

translations.literal: Altair is sacrificial cattle; to its north is River Drum.
translations.idiomatic: Altair is sacrificial cattle; to its north is River Drum.

translations.literal: The great River Drum star is Upper General.
translations.idiomatic: The great River Drum star is Upper General.

translations.literal: Left is Left General.
translations.idiomatic: Left is Left General.

translations.literal: Right is Right General.
translations.idiomatic: Right is Right General.

translations.literal: Wu Maiden, and north of it Weaving Maiden.
translations.idiomatic: Wu Maiden, and north of it Weaving Maiden.

translations.literal: Weaving Maiden is Heaven's granddaughter.
translations.idiomatic: Weaving Maiden is Heaven's granddaughter.

sentences.zh: 歲星曰東方春木，於人五常仁也，五事貌也。

translations.literal: Jupiter is eastern spring wood; in humans it is the constant of benevolence and the affair of appearance.
translations.idiomatic: Jupiter is eastern spring wood; in humans it is the constant of benevolence and the affair of appearance.

sentences.zh: 仁虧貌失，逆春令，傷木氣，罰見歲星。

translations.literal: If benevolence declines and appearance is lost, spring ordinances are violated, wood qi is harmed, and punishment appears through Jupiter.
translations.idiomatic: If benevolence declines and appearance is lost, spring ordinances are violated, wood qi is harmed, and punishment appears through Jupiter.

sentences.zh: 歲星所在，國不可伐，可以伐人。

translations.literal: Where Jupiter is, that state cannot be attacked, but may attack others.
translations.idiomatic: Where Jupiter is, that state cannot be attacked, but may attack others.

sentences.zh: 超舍而前為贏，退舍為縮。

translations.literal: Skipping a lodge forward is called advance; retreating a lodge is called contraction.
translations.idiomatic: Skipping a lodge forward is called advance; retreating a lodge is called contraction.

translations.literal: On advance, that state has troops that do not return.
translations.idiomatic: On advance, that state has troops that do not return.

sentences.zh: 縮，其國有憂，其將死，國傾敗。

translations.literal: On contraction, that state has anxiety; its general dies, and state inclines to defeat.
translations.idiomatic: On contraction, that state has anxiety; its general dies, and state inclines to defeat.

translations.literal: Where it departs, land is lost.
translations.idiomatic: Where it departs, land is lost.

translations.literal: Where it goes, land is gained.
translations.idiomatic: Where it goes, land is gained.

translations.literal: One saying: if it fails to stay where it should stay, state perishes.
translations.idiomatic: One saying: if it fails to stay where it should stay, state perishes.

translations.literal: Where it goes, state prospers.
translations.idiomatic: Where it goes, state prospers.

sentences.zh: 已居之，又東西去之，國凶，不可舉事用兵。

translations.literal: If after staying it again moves east or west away, the state is inauspicious and must not undertake affairs or use troops.
translations.idiomatic: If after staying it again moves east or west away, the state is inauspicious and must not undertake affairs or use troops.

translations.literal: If calm and centered in proper measure, auspicious.
translations.idiomatic: If calm and centered in proper measure, auspicious.

sentences.zh: 出入不當其次，必有天祅見其舍也。

translations.literal: If entering and leaving out of proper sequence, heavenly anomaly must appear in its lodge.
translations.idiomatic: If entering and leaving out of proper sequence, heavenly anomaly must appear in its lodge.

sentences.zh: 歲星贏而東南，石氏「見彗星」，甘氏「不出三月乃生彗，本類星，末類彗，長二丈」。

translations.literal: If Jupiter advances to southeast, Shi school says "a comet appears"; Gan school says "within three months Heavenly Broom is born, root like a star and tail like a broom, two zhang long."
translations.idiomatic: If Jupiter advances to southeast, Shi school says "a comet appears"; Gan school says "within three months Heavenly Broom is born, root like a star and tail like a broom, two zhang long."

sentences.zh: 贏東北，石氏「見覺星」，甘氏「不出三月乃生天棓，本類星，末銳，長四尺」。

translations.literal: If it advances northeast, Shi says "a Jue star appears"; Gan says "within three months Heavenly Baton is born, root like a star and sharp tail, four chi long."
translations.idiomatic: If it advances northeast, Shi says "a Jue star appears"; Gan says "within three months Heavenly Baton is born, root like a star and sharp tail, four chi long."

translations.literal: If it contracts southwest, Shi says
translations.idiomatic: If it contracts southwest, Shi says

sentences.zh: 見欃雲，如牛」，甘氏「不出三月乃生天槍，左右銳，長數丈」。

translations.literal: "a can cloud appears, like an ox"; Gan says "within three months Heavenly Spear is born, sharp on both sides, several zhang long."
translations.idiomatic: "a can cloud appears, like an ox"; Gan says "within three months Heavenly Spear is born, sharp on both sides, several zhang long."

sentences.zh: 縮西北，石氏「見槍雲，如馬」，甘氏「不出三月乃生天欃，本類星，末銳，長數丈」。

translations.literal: If it contracts northwest, Shi says "a spear cloud appears, like a horse"; Gan says "within three months Heavenly Can is born, root like a star and sharp tail, several zhang long."
translations.idiomatic: If it contracts northwest, Shi says "a spear cloud appears, like a horse"; Gan says "within three months Heavenly Can is born, root like a star and sharp tail, several zhang long."

sentences.zh: 石氏「槍、欃、棓、彗異狀，其殃一也，必有破國亂君，伏死其辜，餘殃不盡，為旱、凶、飢、暴疾」。

translations.literal: Shi says: "Spear, Can, Baton, and Broom differ in form but their calamity is one: state-breaking and ruler-disordering, hidden deaths for crimes; remaining disaster not exhausted becomes drought, famine, hunger, and sudden disease."
translations.idiomatic: Shi says: "Spear, Can, Baton, and Broom differ in form but their calamity is one: state-breaking and ruler-disordering, hidden deaths for crimes; remaining disaster not exhausted becomes drought, famine, hunger, and sudden disease."

sentences.zh: 至日行一尺，出二十餘日乃入，甘氏「其國凶，不可舉事用兵」。

translations.literal: When it reaches one chi per day and only enters after more than twenty days, Gan says: "that state is inauspicious and must not undertake affairs or use troops."
translations.idiomatic: When it reaches one chi per day and only enters after more than twenty days, Gan says: "that state is inauspicious and must not undertake affairs or use troops."

sentences.zh: 出而易，「所當之國，是受其殃」。

translations.literal: If it emerges and changes, "the state it points to receives that calamity."
translations.idiomatic: If it emerges and changes, "the state it points to receives that calamity."

sentences.zh: 又曰「祅星，不出三年，其下有軍，及失地，若國君喪」。

translations.literal: Also: "If anomalous star does not emerge for three years, below it there will be armies, territorial loss, or ruler's death."
translations.idiomatic: Also: "If anomalous star does not emerge for three years, below it there will be armies, territorial loss, or ruler's death."

sentences.zh: 熒惑曰南方夏火，禮也，視也。

translations.literal: Mars is southern summer fire; ritual and sight.
translations.idiomatic: Mars is southern summer fire; ritual and sight.

sentences.zh: 禮虧視失，逆夏令，傷火氣，罰見熒惑。

translations.literal: If ritual declines and sight fails, summer ordinances are violated, fire qi is harmed, and punishment appears through Mars.
translations.idiomatic: If ritual declines and sight fails, summer ordinances are violated, fire qi is harmed, and punishment appears through Mars.

sentences.zh: 逆行一舍二舍為不祥，居之三月國有殃，五月受兵，七月國半亡地，九月地太半亡。

translations.literal: Retrograde one or two lodges is inauspicious; if it dwells three months the state suffers, at five months it receives warfare, at seven months the state loses half its land, at nine months more than half.
translations.idiomatic: Retrograde one or two lodges is inauspicious; if it dwells three months the state suffers, at five months it receives warfare, at seven months the state loses half its land, at nine months more than half.

translations.literal: If it enters and exits together with another body, the state cuts off its sacrifices.
translations.idiomatic: If it enters and exits together with another body, the state cuts off its sacrifices.

translations.literal: Mars signifies disorder and
translations.idiomatic: Mars signifies disorder and

sentences.zh: 成，為疾為喪，為飢為兵，所居之宿國受殃。

translations.literal: completion, disease and mourning, hunger and war; the state of the lodge where it dwells suffers calamity.
translations.idiomatic: completion, disease and mourning, hunger and war; the state of the lodge where it dwells suffers calamity.

translations.literal: If calamity returns immediately, though great it should be read as small.
translations.idiomatic: If calamity returns immediately, though great it should be read as small.

sentences.zh: 居之久殃乃至者，當小反大。

translations.literal: If it dwells long and calamity arrives later, what seems small reverses into great.
translations.idiomatic: If it dwells long and calamity arrives later, what seems small reverses into great.

sentences.zh: 已去復還居之，若居之而角者，若動者，繞環之，及乍前乍後，乍左乍右，殃愈甚。

translations.literal: If after leaving it returns and dwells again, or while dwelling it horns, or moves, or circles around, or suddenly goes forward and back, left and right, the calamity is ever more severe.
translations.idiomatic: If after leaving it returns and dwells again, or while dwelling it horns, or moves, or circles around, or suddenly goes forward and back, left and right, the calamity is ever more severe.

sentences.zh: 一曰，熒惑出則有大兵，入則兵散。

translations.literal: One saying: when Mars emerges, there are great armies; when it enters, armies disperse.
translations.idiomatic: One saying: when Mars emerges, there are great armies; when it enters, armies disperse.

translations.literal: Only when it completes a circuit and comes to rest do deaths and mourning occur.
translations.idiomatic: Only when it completes a circuit and comes to rest do deaths and mourning occur.

sentences.zh: 寇亂在其野者亡地，以戰不勝。

translations.literal: Where invasion and disorder are in its field, land is lost and war is not won.
translations.idiomatic: Where invasion and disorder are in its field, land is lost and war is not won.

sentences.zh: 東行疾則兵聚于東方，西行疾則兵聚于西方；

translations.literal: If it moves east swiftly, armies gather in the east; if it moves west swiftly, armies gather in the west.
translations.idiomatic: If it moves east swiftly, armies gather in the east; if it moves west swiftly, armies gather in the west.

sentences.zh: 其南為丈夫喪，北為女子喪。

translations.literal: To its south means mourning of men; to its north mourning of women.
translations.idiomatic: To its south means mourning of men; to its north mourning of women.

sentences.zh: 熒惑，天子理也，故曰雖有明天子，必視熒惑所在。

translations.literal: Mars is the Son of Heaven's principle of governance; therefore it is said: even with an enlightened Son of Heaven, Mars's position must be watched.
translations.idiomatic: Mars is the Son of Heaven's principle of governance; therefore it is said: even with an enlightened Son of Heaven, Mars's position must be watched.

sentences.zh: 太白曰西方秋金，義也，言也。

translations.literal: Venus is western autumn metal; righteousness and speech.
translations.idiomatic: Venus is western autumn metal; righteousness and speech.

sentences.zh: 義虧言失，逆秋令，傷金氣，罰見太白。

translations.literal: If righteousness declines and speech fails, autumn ordinances are violated, metal qi is harmed, and punishment appears through Venus.
translations.idiomatic: If righteousness declines and speech fails, autumn ordinances are violated, metal qi is harmed, and punishment appears through Venus.

sentences.zh: 日方南太白居其南，日方北太白居其北，為贏，侯王不寧，用兵進吉退凶。

translations.literal: If the sun is south and Venus is south of it, or the sun is north and Venus north of it, this is advance: feudal rulers are unsettled; in warfare, advancing is auspicious and retreating inauspicious.
translations.idiomatic: If the sun is south and Venus is south of it, or the sun is north and Venus north of it, this is advance: feudal rulers are unsettled; in warfare, advancing is auspicious and retreating inauspicious.

sentences.zh: 日方南太白居其北，日方北太白居其南，為縮，侯王有憂，用兵退吉進凶。

translations.literal: If the sun is south and Venus north of it, or the sun is north and Venus south of it, this is contraction: feudal rulers have anxiety; in warfare, retreat is auspicious and advance inauspicious.
translations.idiomatic: If the sun is south and Venus north of it, or the sun is north and Venus south of it, this is contraction: feudal rulers have anxiety; in warfare, retreat is auspicious and advance inauspicious.

sentences.zh: 當出不出，當入不入，為失舍，不有破軍，必有死王之墓，有亡國。

translations.literal: If it should emerge but does not, or should enter but does not, this is losing its lodge; if there is no shattered army, there will be tombs of dead kings and ruined states.
translations.idiomatic: If it should emerge but does not, or should enter but does not, this is losing its lodge; if there is no shattered army, there will be tombs of dead kings and ruined states.

sentences.zh: 一曰，天下匽兵，野有兵者，所當之國大凶。

translations.literal: One saying: under heaven armies are halted; if there are field armies, the state it points to is greatly inauspicious.
translations.idiomatic: One saying: under heaven armies are halted; if there are field armies, the state it points to is greatly inauspicious.

sentences.zh: 當出不出，未當入而入，天下匽兵，兵在外，入。

translations.literal: If it should emerge but does not, and enters before it should, armies under heaven are halted; troops in the field return inward.
translations.idiomatic: If it should emerge but does not, and enters before it should, armies under heaven are halted; troops in the field return inward.

sentences.zh: 未當出而出，當入而不入，天下起兵，有至破國。

translations.literal: If it emerges before it should, and when it should enter it does not, armies rise under heaven, and some states are broken.
translations.idiomatic: If it emerges before it should, and when it should enter it does not, armies rise under heaven, and some states are broken.

sentences.zh: 未當出而出，未當入而入，天下舉兵，所當之國亡。

translations.literal: If it emerges before due and enters before due, armies are raised under heaven; the state it points to perishes.
translations.idiomatic: If it emerges before due and enters before due, armies are raised under heaven; the state it points to perishes.

translations.literal: If it emerges on schedule, that state prospers.
translations.idiomatic: If it emerges on schedule, that state prospers.

translations.literal: Emerging east corresponds to east; entering corresponds to north.
translations.idiomatic: Emerging east corresponds to east; entering corresponds to north.

translations.literal: Emerging west corresponds to west; entering corresponds to south.
translations.idiomatic: Emerging west corresponds to west; entering corresponds to south.

translations.literal: If it dwells long, that state benefits.
translations.idiomatic: If it dwells long, that state benefits.

translations.literal: If it shifts, its region is inauspicious.
translations.idiomatic: If it shifts, its region is inauspicious.

translations.literal: If it enters and re-emerges within seven days, the general dies in battle.
translations.idiomatic: If it enters and re-emerges within seven days, the general dies in battle.

translations.literal: If it enters and re-emerges within ten days, the chancellor dies.
translations.idiomatic: If it enters and re-emerges within ten days, the chancellor dies.

translations.literal: If it enters and again re-emerges, rulers abhor it.
translations.idiomatic: If it enters and again re-emerges, rulers abhor it.

sentences.zh: 已出三日而復微入，三日乃復盛出，是為耎而伏，其下國有軍，其眾敗將北。

translations.literal: If after emergence, by day three it faintly re-enters, and by another three days re-emerges bright, this is called soft-and-submerged: in the state below there is war, its forces are defeated and its generals routed.
translations.idiomatic: If after emergence, by day three it faintly re-enters, and by another three days re-emerges bright, this is called soft-and-submerged: in the state below there is war, its forces are defeated and its generals routed.

sentences.zh: 已入三日，又復微出，三日乃復盛入，其下國有憂，帥師雖眾，敵食其糧，用其兵，虜其帥。

translations.literal: If after entry, by day three it faintly re-emerges, and by another three days enters brightly again, in the state below there is anxiety; even with many troops, enemies eat its grain, use its troops, and capture its commander.
translations.idiomatic: If after entry, by day three it faintly re-emerges, and by another three days enters brightly again, in the state below there is anxiety; even with many troops, enemies eat its grain, use its troops, and capture its commander.

translations.literal: If it emerges in west and loses its path, the barbarians are defeated.
translations.idiomatic: If it emerges in west and loses its path, the barbarians are defeated.

translations.literal: If it emerges in east and loses its path, the central states are defeated.
translations.idiomatic: If it emerges in east and loses its path, the central states are defeated.

sentences.zh: 一曰，出蚤為月食，晚為天祅及彗星，將發於亡道之國。

translations.literal: One saying: early emergence means lunar eclipse; late means heavenly anomaly and comet, soon to arise in a state that has lost the Way.
translations.idiomatic: One saying: early emergence means lunar eclipse; late means heavenly anomaly and comet, soon to arise in a state that has lost the Way.

sentences.zh: 太白出而留桑榆間，病其下國。

translations.literal: If Venus emerges and lingers between mulberry and elm stars, the state below suffers illness.
translations.idiomatic: If Venus emerges and lingers between mulberry and elm stars, the state below suffers illness.

sentences.zh: 上而疾，未盡期日過參天，病其對國。

translations.literal: If it rises and moves swiftly, before due term passes beyond Shen culmination, the opposing state suffers illness.
translations.idiomatic: If it rises and moves swiftly, before due term passes beyond Shen culmination, the opposing state suffers illness.

sentences.zh: 太白經天，天下革，民更王，是為亂紀，人民流亡。

translations.literal: If Venus crosses the sky, the realm changes regime; people replace kings. This is disorder of chronology, with people displaced and scattered.
translations.idiomatic: If Venus crosses the sky, the realm changes regime; people replace kings. This is disorder of chronology, with people displaced and scattered.

sentences.zh: 晝見與日爭明，彊國弱，小國彊，女主昌。

translations.literal: If seen by day competing in brightness with the sun, strong states weaken, small states strengthen, and female rule prospers.
translations.idiomatic: If seen by day competing in brightness with the sun, strong states weaken, small states strengthen, and female rule prospers.

translations.literal: Venus is the emblem of war.
translations.idiomatic: Venus is the emblem of war.

translations.literal: If it emerges high, deep offensives are auspicious and shallow ones inauspicious.
translations.idiomatic: If it emerges high, deep offensives are auspicious and shallow ones inauspicious.

translations.literal: If low, shallow offensives are auspicious and deep ones inauspicious.
translations.idiomatic: If low, shallow offensives are auspicious and deep ones inauspicious.

translations.literal: If it moves fast, rapid operations are auspicious and slow ones inauspicious.
translations.idiomatic: If it moves fast, rapid operations are auspicious and slow ones inauspicious.

translations.literal: If it moves slowly, slow operations are auspicious and rapid ones inauspicious.
translations.idiomatic: If it moves slowly, slow operations are auspicious and rapid ones inauspicious.

translations.literal: If it horns, daring battle is auspicious and avoiding battle inauspicious.
translations.idiomatic: If it horns, daring battle is auspicious and avoiding battle inauspicious.

translations.literal: Striking where its horns point is auspicious; opposing that direction is inauspicious.
translations.idiomatic: Striking where its horns point is auspicious; opposing that direction is inauspicious.

sentences.zh: 進退左右，用兵進退左右吉，靜凶。

translations.literal: If it advances, retreats, shifts left or right, matching those maneuvers in war is auspicious; staying still is inauspicious.
translations.idiomatic: If it advances, retreats, shifts left or right, matching those maneuvers in war is auspicious; staying still is inauspicious.

translations.literal: If it is round and still, stillness in war is auspicious and haste inauspicious.
translations.idiomatic: If it is round and still, stillness in war is auspicious and haste inauspicious.

translations.literal: When it emerges, troops go out; when it enters, troops come in.
translations.idiomatic: When it emerges, troops go out; when it enters, troops come in.

translations.literal: To imitate Venus is auspicious; to reverse it is inauspicious.
translations.idiomatic: To imitate Venus is auspicious; to reverse it is inauspicious.

translations.literal: If its horns are red, there is battle.
translations.idiomatic: If its horns are red, there is battle.

sentences.zh: 太白者，猶軍也，而熒惑，憂也。

translations.literal: Venus is like an army, while Mars is anxiety.
translations.idiomatic: Venus is like an army, while Mars is anxiety.

translations.literal: Therefore when Mars follows Venus, the army is anxious.
translations.idiomatic: Therefore when Mars follows Venus, the army is anxious.

translations.literal: When it separates from Venus, the army relaxes.
translations.idiomatic: When it separates from Venus, the army relaxes.

translations.literal: If it emerges on Venus's shaded side, armies divide.
translations.idiomatic: If it emerges on Venus's shaded side, armies divide.

translations.literal: If it emerges on Venus's lit side, there is battle by detached commanders.
translations.idiomatic: If it emerges on Venus's lit side, there is battle by detached commanders.

sentences.zh: 當其行，太白還之，破軍殺將。

translations.literal: When on its path Venus turns back against it, armies are broken and generals killed.
translations.idiomatic: When on its path Venus turns back against it, armies are broken and generals killed.

sentences.zh: 辰星，殺伐之氣，戰鬥之象也。

translations.literal: Mercury is the qi of killing and cutting, emblem of combat.
translations.idiomatic: Mercury is the qi of killing and cutting, emblem of combat.

sentences.zh: 與太白俱出東方，皆赤而角，夷狄敗，中國勝；

translations.literal: If it and Venus both emerge in east, both red and horned, barbarians lose and central states win.
translations.idiomatic: If it and Venus both emerge in east, both red and horned, barbarians lose and central states win.

sentences.zh: 與太白俱出西方，皆赤而角，中國敗，夷狄勝。

translations.literal: If both emerge in west, both red and horned, central states lose and barbarians win.
translations.idiomatic: If both emerge in west, both red and horned, central states lose and barbarians win.

sentences.zh: 五星分天之中，積于東方，中國大利；

translations.literal: If five planets divide the middle sky and cluster in east, central states have great advantage.
translations.idiomatic: If five planets divide the middle sky and cluster in east, central states have great advantage.

sentences.zh: 積于西方，夷狄用兵者利。

translations.literal: If clustered in west, those using arms among barbarians gain advantage.
translations.idiomatic: If clustered in west, those using arms among barbarians gain advantage.

translations.literal: If Mercury does not emerge, Venus is guest.
translations.idiomatic: If Mercury does not emerge, Venus is guest.

translations.literal: If Mercury emerges, Venus is host.
translations.idiomatic: If Mercury emerges, Venus is host.

sentences.zh: 辰星與太白不相從，雖有軍不戰。

translations.literal: If Mercury and Venus do not move together, even with armies there is no battle.
translations.idiomatic: If Mercury and Venus do not move together, even with armies there is no battle.

sentences.zh: 辰星出東方，太白出西方。

translations.literal: If Mercury emerges east and Venus west.
translations.idiomatic: If Mercury emerges east and Venus west.

sentences.zh: 若辰星出西方，太白出東方，為格，野雖有兵，不戰。

translations.literal: Or if Mercury emerges west and Venus east, this is obstruction: though armies are in the field, no battle occurs.
translations.idiomatic: Or if Mercury emerges west and Venus east, this is obstruction: though armies are in the field, no battle occurs.

sentences.zh: 辰星入太白中，五日乃出，及入而上出，破軍殺將，客勝；

translations.literal: If Mercury enters within Venus and emerges after five days, or enters and emerges above, armies are broken and generals killed; the guest side wins.
translations.idiomatic: If Mercury enters within Venus and emerges after five days, or enters and emerges above, armies are broken and generals killed; the guest side wins.

translations.literal: If it emerges below, guest loses territory.
translations.idiomatic: If it emerges below, guest loses territory.

sentences.zh: 辰星來抵，太白不去，將死。

translations.literal: If Mercury comes up against Venus and Venus does not withdraw, the general dies.
translations.idiomatic: If Mercury comes up against Venus and Venus does not withdraw, the general dies.

sentences.zh: 正其上出，破軍殺將，客勝；

translations.literal: If it emerges directly above, armies are broken, generals killed, and guest wins.
translations.idiomatic: If it emerges directly above, armies are broken, generals killed, and guest wins.

translations.literal: If it emerges below, guest loses territory.
translations.idiomatic: If it emerges below, guest loses territory.

translations.literal: Judge by where it points to name the army break.
translations.idiomatic: Judge by where it points to name the army break.

sentences.zh: 辰星繞環太白，若鬥，大戰，客勝，主人吏死。

translations.literal: If Mercury circles Venus as if fighting, there is great battle, guest wins, and host officials die.
translations.idiomatic: If Mercury circles Venus as if fighting, there is great battle, guest wins, and host officials die.

sentences.zh: 辰星過太白，間可椷劍，小戰，客勝；

translations.literal: If Mercury passes Venus at a gap no wider than a sheathed sword, there is small battle and guest wins.
translations.idiomatic: If Mercury passes Venus at a gap no wider than a sheathed sword, there is small battle and guest wins.

translations.literal: If it stays before Venus for ten days and three, armies withdraw.
translations.idiomatic: If it stays before Venus for ten days and three, armies withdraw.

translations.literal: If it emerges left of Venus, small battle.
translations.idiomatic: If it emerges left of Venus, small battle.

sentences.zh: 歷太白右，數萬人戰，主人吏死；

translations.literal: If it passes Venus's right, tens of thousands fight and host officials die.
translations.idiomatic: If it passes Venus's right, tens of thousands fight and host officials die.

sentences.zh: 出太白右，去三尺，軍急約戰。

translations.literal: If it emerges right of Venus and departs by three chi, armies urgently agree to battle.
translations.idiomatic: If it emerges right of Venus and departs by three chi, armies urgently agree to battle.

sentences.zh: 凡太白所出所直之辰，其國為得位，得位者戰勝。

translations.literal: In general, on the day-stem aligned with where Venus emerges or points, that state obtains position; the side with position wins battle.
translations.idiomatic: In general, on the day-stem aligned with where Venus emerges or points, that state obtains position; the side with position wins battle.

sentences.zh: 所直之辰順其色而角者勝，其色害者敗。

translations.literal: If the day-stem Venus points to accords in color and horns, it wins; if color is harmed, it loses.
translations.idiomatic: If the day-stem Venus points to accords in color and horns, it wins; if color is harmed, it loses.

sentences.zh: 太白白比狼，赤比心，黃比參右肩，青比參左肩，黑比奎大星。

translations.literal: Venus white corresponds to Wolf; red to Heart; yellow to Shen's right shoulder; blue to Shen's left shoulder; black to Kui's great star.
translations.idiomatic: Venus white corresponds to Wolf; red to Heart; yellow to Shen's right shoulder; blue to Shen's left shoulder; black to Kui's great star.

sentences.zh: 色勝位，行勝色，行得盡勝之。

translations.literal: Color overcomes position, movement overcomes color, and if movement gains full advantage it overcomes all.
translations.idiomatic: Color overcomes position, movement overcomes color, and if movement gains full advantage it overcomes all.

sentences.zh: 辰星曰北方冬水，知也，聽也。

translations.literal: Mercury is northern winter water: knowledge and hearing.
translations.idiomatic: Mercury is northern winter water: knowledge and hearing.

sentences.zh: 知虧聽失，逆冬令，傷水氣，罰見辰星。

translations.literal: If knowledge declines and hearing fails, winter ordinances are violated, water qi harmed, and punishment appears through Mercury.
translations.idiomatic: If knowledge declines and hearing fails, winter ordinances are violated, water qi harmed, and punishment appears through Mercury.

sentences.zh: 出蚤為月食，晚為彗星及天祅。

translations.literal: If it emerges early, lunar eclipse; if late, comet and heavenly anomaly.
translations.idiomatic: If it emerges early, lunar eclipse; if late, comet and heavenly anomaly.

translations.literal: If in one season it does not emerge, that season is disharmonious.
translations.idiomatic: If in one season it does not emerge, that season is disharmonious.

translations.literal: If in all four seasons it does not emerge, the world suffers great famine.
translations.idiomatic: If in all four seasons it does not emerge, the world suffers great famine.

sentences.zh: 失其時而出，為當寒反溫，當溫反寒。

translations.literal: If it emerges out of season, when cold should prevail it turns warm, when warm should prevail it turns cold.
translations.idiomatic: If it emerges out of season, when cold should prevail it turns warm, when warm should prevail it turns cold.

sentences.zh: 當出不出，是謂擊卒，兵大起。

translations.literal: If it should emerge but does not, this is called striking the ranks; great armies arise.
translations.idiomatic: If it should emerge but does not, this is called striking the ranks; great armies arise.

sentences.zh: 與它星遇而鬥，天下大亂。

translations.literal: If it meets and fights other stars, there is great disorder under heaven.
translations.idiomatic: If it meets and fights other stars, there is great disorder under heaven.

translations.literal: If it emerges between Fang and Xin, there is earthquake.
translations.idiomatic: If it emerges between Fang and Xin, there is earthquake.

sentences.zh: 填星曰中央季夏土，信也，思心也。

translations.literal: Saturn is central late-summer earth: trustworthiness and thought-heart.
translations.idiomatic: Saturn is central late-summer earth: trustworthiness and thought-heart.

sentences.zh: 仁義禮智以信為主，貌言視聽以心為正，故四星皆失，填星乃為之動。

translations.literal: Among benevolence, righteousness, ritual, and wisdom, trust is the master; among appearance, speech, sight, and hearing, heart-mind is the standard; therefore when four stars all fail, Saturn moves in response.
translations.idiomatic: Among benevolence, righteousness, ritual, and wisdom, trust is the master; among appearance, speech, sight, and hearing, heart-mind is the standard; therefore when four stars all fail, Saturn moves in response.

translations.literal: Where Saturn resides, that state is auspicious.
translations.idiomatic: Where Saturn resides, that state is auspicious.

sentences.zh: 未當居而居之，若已去而復還居之，國得土，不乃得女子。

translations.literal: If it resides where it should not, or after leaving returns to reside, the state gains land, and may also gain women.
translations.idiomatic: If it resides where it should not, or after leaving returns to reside, the state gains land, and may also gain women.

sentences.zh: 當居不居，既已居之，又東西去之，國失土，不乃失女，不，有土事若女之憂。

translations.literal: If it should reside but does not, or after residing then departs east or west, the state loses land, and may lose women, or have land disputes or women's worries.
translations.idiomatic: If it should reside but does not, or after residing then departs east or west, the state loses land, and may lose women, or have land disputes or women's worries.

translations.literal: If it stays long in one lodge, that state's blessings are deep.
translations.idiomatic: If it stays long in one lodge, that state's blessings are deep.

translations.literal: If it shifts, blessings are thin.
translations.idiomatic: If it shifts, blessings are thin.

sentences.zh: 當居不居，為失填，其下國可伐；

translations.literal: If it should reside but does not, this is loss of Saturn; states below may be attacked.
translations.idiomatic: If it should reside but does not, this is loss of Saturn; states below may be attacked.

translations.literal: Those that have position may not be attacked.
translations.idiomatic: Those that have position may not be attacked.

translations.literal: Its advance means the king is unsettled.
translations.idiomatic: Its advance means the king is unsettled.

translations.literal: Its contraction means armies do not return.
translations.idiomatic: Its contraction means armies do not return.

sentences.zh: 一曰，既已居之又東西去之，其國凶，不可舉事用兵。

translations.literal: One saying: if after residing it departs east or west, that state is inauspicious and must not undertake affairs or use troops.
translations.idiomatic: One saying: if after residing it departs east or west, that state is inauspicious and must not undertake affairs or use troops.

sentences.zh: 失次而上一舍三舍，有王命不成，不乃大水；

translations.literal: If it misses sequence and rises one to three lodges, royal commands fail, or there is great flood.
translations.idiomatic: If it misses sequence and rises one to three lodges, royal commands fail, or there is great flood.

sentences.zh: 失次而下二舍，有后慼，其歲不復，不乃天裂若地動。

translations.literal: If it misses sequence and drops two lodges, there is distress among consorts; that year things do not recover, or there is sky-rift or earthquake.
translations.idiomatic: If it misses sequence and drops two lodges, there is distress among consorts; that year things do not recover, or there is sky-rift or earthquake.

sentences.zh: 凡五星，歲與填合則為內亂，與辰合則為變謀而更事，與熒惑合則為飢，為旱，與太白合則為白衣之會，為水。

translations.literal: In general for five planets: if Jupiter and Saturn join there is internal disorder; if Jupiter and Mercury join there are plots and policy changes; if Jupiter and Mars join there is famine and drought; if Jupiter and Venus join there are assemblies in white clothing and floods.
translations.idiomatic: In general for five planets: if Jupiter and Saturn join there is internal disorder; if Jupiter and Mercury join there are plots and policy changes; if Jupiter and Mars join there is famine and drought; if Jupiter and Venus join there are assemblies in white clothing and floods.

sentences.zh: 太白在南，歲在北，名曰牡牡，年穀大孰。

translations.literal: If Venus is south and Jupiter north, this is called mu-mu; the year's grain greatly ripens.
translations.idiomatic: If Venus is south and Jupiter north, this is called mu-mu; the year's grain greatly ripens.

sentences.zh: 太白在北，歲在南，年或有或亡。

translations.literal: If Venus north and Jupiter south, the year may have harvest or may fail.
translations.idiomatic: If Venus north and Jupiter south, the year may have harvest or may fail.

sentences.zh: 熒惑與太白合則為喪，不可舉事用兵；

translations.literal: If Mars joins Venus, there is mourning, and no affairs or military action should be undertaken.
translations.idiomatic: If Mars joins Venus, there is mourning, and no affairs or military action should be undertaken.

translations.literal: If it joins Saturn, there is anxiety, chiefly among high ministers.
translations.idiomatic: If it joins Saturn, there is anxiety, chiefly among high ministers.

sentences.zh: 與辰合則為北軍，用兵舉事大敗。

translations.literal: If it joins Mercury, there is northern-army omen, and military enterprises suffer great defeat.
translations.idiomatic: If it joins Mercury, there is northern-army omen, and military enterprises suffer great defeat.

sentences.zh: 填與辰合則將有覆軍下師；

translations.literal: If Saturn joins Mercury, there will be overturned armies and lost divisions.
translations.idiomatic: If Saturn joins Mercury, there will be overturned armies and lost divisions.

sentences.zh: 與太白合則為疾，為內兵。

translations.literal: If it joins Venus, there is sickness and internal warfare.
translations.idiomatic: If it joins Venus, there is sickness and internal warfare.

sentences.zh: 辰與太白合則為變謀，為兵憂。

translations.literal: If Mercury joins Venus, there are plot changes and military anxiety.
translations.idiomatic: If Mercury joins Venus, there are plot changes and military anxiety.

sentences.zh: 凡歲、熒惑、填、太白四星與辰鬥，皆為戰，兵不在外，皆為內亂。

translations.literal: In general, when Jupiter, Mars, Saturn, and Venus each contend with Mercury, all signify war; if troops are not abroad, all signify internal disorder.
translations.idiomatic: In general, when Jupiter, Mars, Saturn, and Venus each contend with Mercury, all signify war; if troops are not abroad, all signify internal disorder.

sentences.zh: 一曰，火與水合為淬，與金合為鑠，不可舉事用兵。

translations.literal: One saying: when Mars joins Mercury it is quenching; when Mars joins Venus it is melting - no affairs or military action should be undertaken.
translations.idiomatic: One saying: when Mars joins Mercury it is quenching; when Mars joins Venus it is melting - no affairs or military action should be undertaken.

sentences.zh: 土與金合國亡地，與木合則國饑，與水合為雍沮，不可舉事用兵。

translations.literal: When Saturn joins Venus, states lose territory; when it joins Jupiter, states suffer famine; when it joins Mercury, there is blockage and stagnation - no affairs or military action should be undertaken.
translations.idiomatic: When Saturn joins Venus, states lose territory; when it joins Jupiter, states suffer famine; when it joins Mercury, there is blockage and stagnation - no affairs or military action should be undertaken.

translations.literal: When Jupiter joins Venus in contention, the state has internal disorder.
translations.idiomatic: When Jupiter joins Venus in contention, the state has internal disorder.

translations.literal: Sharing one lodge is called conjunction; mutually overstepping is called contention.
translations.idiomatic: Sharing one lodge is called conjunction; mutually overstepping is called contention.

sentences.zh: 二星相近者其殃大，二星相遠者殃無傷也，從七寸以內必之。

translations.literal: If two planets are close, the calamity is great; if far apart, harm is slight. Within seven cun, it certainly manifests.
translations.idiomatic: If two planets are close, the calamity is great; if far apart, harm is slight. Within seven cun, it certainly manifests.

sentences.zh: 凡月食五星，其國必亡：歲以飢，熒惑以亂，填以殺，太白彊國以戰，辰以女亂。

translations.literal: Whenever moon eclipses one of five planets, that state must perish: Jupiter means famine, Mars disorder, Saturn killing, Venus means strong states making war, Mercury means disorder through women.
translations.idiomatic: Whenever moon eclipses one of five planets, that state must perish: Jupiter means famine, Mars disorder, Saturn killing, Venus means strong states making war, Mercury means disorder through women.

translations.literal: If the moon eclipses Great Horn, rulers should dread it.
translations.idiomatic: If the moon eclipses Great Horn, rulers should dread it.

sentences.zh: 凡五星所聚宿，其國王天下：從歲以義，從熒惑以禮，從填以重，從太白以兵，從辰以法。

translations.literal: Whenever five planets cluster in one lodge, that state's ruler will rule all under Heaven: following Jupiter by righteousness, Mars by ritual, Saturn by gravity, Venus by military force, Mercury by law.
translations.idiomatic: Whenever five planets cluster in one lodge, that state's ruler will rule all under Heaven: following Jupiter by righteousness, Mars by ritual, Saturn by gravity, Venus by military force, Mercury by law.

translations.literal: By law means winning all under Heaven through law.
translations.idiomatic: By law means winning all under Heaven through law.

sentences.zh: 三星若合，是謂驚立絕行，其國外內有兵與喪，民人乏飢，改立王公。

translations.literal: If three stars seem conjoined, this is called startled stand and broken movement: inside and outside that state there are armies and mourning, people are hungry and destitute, and kings or nobles are replaced.
translations.idiomatic: If three stars seem conjoined, this is called startled stand and broken movement: inside and outside that state there are armies and mourning, people are hungry and destitute, and kings or nobles are replaced.

sentences.zh: 四星若合，是謂大湯，其國兵喪並起，君子憂，小人流。

translations.literal: If four stars seem conjoined, this is called great boiling: warfare and mourning arise together in that state; gentlemen are anxious and commoners drift away.
translations.idiomatic: If four stars seem conjoined, this is called great boiling: warfare and mourning arise together in that state; gentlemen are anxious and commoners drift away.

sentences.zh: 五星若合，是謂易行：有德受慶，改立王者，掩有四方，子孫蕃昌；

translations.literal: If five stars conjoin, this is called changed movement: one with virtue receives blessing, a new ruler is established, and the four quarters are brought under cover, with descendants flourishing.
translations.idiomatic: If five stars conjoin, this is called changed movement: one with virtue receives blessing, a new ruler is established, and the four quarters are brought under cover, with descendants flourishing.

sentences.zh: 亡德受罰，離其國家，滅其宗廟，百姓離去，被滿四方。

translations.literal: One without virtue receives punishment, is separated from his state and house, has ancestral temples destroyed, and people scatter to the four directions.
translations.idiomatic: One without virtue receives punishment, is separated from his state and house, has ancestral temples destroyed, and people scatter to the four directions.

translations.literal: If all five stars are large, the matter is large.
translations.idiomatic: If all five stars are large, the matter is large.

translations.literal: If all are small, the matter is small.
translations.idiomatic: If all are small, the matter is small.

sentences.zh: 凡五星色：皆圜，白為喪為旱，赤中不平為兵，青為憂為水，黑為疾為多死，黃吉；

translations.literal: In general, five-star colors: all round - white means mourning and drought; red with uneven center means war; blue means anxiety and flood; black means illness and many deaths; yellow is auspicious.
translations.idiomatic: In general, five-star colors: all round - white means mourning and drought; red with uneven center means war; blue means anxiety and flood; black means illness and many deaths; yellow is auspicious.

sentences.zh: 皆角，赤犯我城，黃地之爭，白哭泣之聲，青有兵憂，黑水。

translations.literal: If all are horned: red means attack on our city, yellow means land disputes, white means sounds of weeping, blue means military anxiety, black means flood.
translations.idiomatic: If all are horned: red means attack on our city, yellow means land disputes, white means sounds of weeping, blue means military anxiety, black means flood.

sentences.zh: 五星同色，天下匽兵，百姓安寧，歌舞以行，不見災疾，五穀蕃昌。

translations.literal: If the five stars share one color, armies under heaven cease, people are peaceful, song and dance proceed, no disasters or epidemics appear, and the five grains flourish.
translations.idiomatic: If the five stars share one color, armies under heaven cease, people are peaceful, song and dance proceed, no disasters or epidemics appear, and the five grains flourish.

sentences.zh: 凡五星，歲，緩則不行，急則過分，逆則占。

translations.literal: For five planets: with Jupiter, if slow and not moving, or fast and overshooting divisions, or retrograde, interpret omen.
translations.idiomatic: For five planets: with Jupiter, if slow and not moving, or fast and overshooting divisions, or retrograde, interpret omen.

sentences.zh: 熒惑，緩則不出，急則不入，違道則占。

translations.literal: For Mars, if slow and not emerging, fast and not entering, or off its path, interpret omen.
translations.idiomatic: For Mars, if slow and not emerging, fast and not entering, or off its path, interpret omen.

sentences.zh: 填，緩則不建，急則過舍，逆則占。

translations.literal: For Saturn, if slow and not establishing, fast and overshooting lodges, or retrograde, interpret omen.
translations.idiomatic: For Saturn, if slow and not establishing, fast and overshooting lodges, or retrograde, interpret omen.

sentences.zh: 太白，緩則不出，急則不入，逆則占。

translations.literal: For Venus, if slow and not emerging, fast and not entering, or retrograde, interpret omen.
translations.idiomatic: For Venus, if slow and not emerging, fast and not entering, or retrograde, interpret omen.

sentences.zh: 辰，緩則不出，急則不入，非時則占。

translations.literal: For Mercury, if slow and not emerging, fast and not entering, or out of season, interpret omen.
translations.idiomatic: For Mercury, if slow and not emerging, fast and not entering, or out of season, interpret omen.

sentences.zh: 五星不失行，則年穀豐昌。

translations.literal: If the five stars do not lose their proper motion, yearly grain is abundant.
translations.idiomatic: If the five stars do not lose their proper motion, yearly grain is abundant.

sentences.zh: 凡以宿星通下之變者，維星散，句星信，則地動。

translations.literal: In general, for changes below inferred from lodge stars: if Wei stars scatter and Gou star confirms, there is earthquake.
translations.idiomatic: In general, for changes below inferred from lodge stars: if Wei stars scatter and Gou star confirms, there is earthquake.

sentences.zh: 有星守三淵，天下大水，地動，海魚出。

translations.literal: If a star stations at the Three Abysses, there is great flood under heaven, earthquake, and sea fish coming out.
translations.idiomatic: If a star stations at the Three Abysses, there is great flood under heaven, earthquake, and sea fish coming out.

sentences.zh: 紀星散者山崩，不即有喪。

translations.literal: If Tianji stars scatter, mountains collapse; if not, there is mourning.
translations.idiomatic: If Tianji stars scatter, mountains collapse; if not, there is mourning.

sentences.zh: 龜、鱉星不居漢中，川有易者。

translations.literal: If Turtle and Turtle-shell stars do not remain in the Milky River center, rivers and streams will alter course.
translations.idiomatic: If Turtle and Turtle-shell stars do not remain in the Milky River center, rivers and streams will alter course.

translations.literal: If Mercury enters Five Chariots, there is great flood.
translations.idiomatic: If Mercury enters Five Chariots, there is great flood.

translations.literal: If Mars enters Stored Waters, there is flood and war rises.
translations.idiomatic: If Mars enters Stored Waters, there is flood and war rises.

translations.literal: If it enters Stored Firewood, drought and war rise.
translations.idiomatic: If it enters Stored Firewood, drought and war rise.

translations.literal: If it stations there, the same.
translations.idiomatic: If it stations there, the same.

translations.literal: Behind the Pole are four stars called Hook Star.
translations.idiomatic: Behind the Pole are four stars called Hook Star.

sentences.zh: 斗杓後有三星，名曰維星。

translations.literal: Behind the Dipper handle are three stars called Dimensional Stars.
translations.idiomatic: Behind the Dipper handle are three stars called Dimensional Stars.

translations.literal: Scattered means they do not follow each other.
translations.idiomatic: Scattered means they do not follow each other.

translations.literal: Three Abysses likely means the three pillars of Five Chariots.
translations.idiomatic: Three Abysses likely means the three pillars of Five Chariots.

translations.literal: Heavenly Cord attaches to Coiled Rope.
translations.idiomatic: Heavenly Cord attaches to Coiled Rope.

translations.literal: Stored Firewood is northwest of northern garrison.
translations.idiomatic: Stored Firewood is northwest of northern garrison.

translations.literal: Stored Waters is northeast of northern garrison.
translations.idiomatic: Stored Waters is northeast of northern garrison.

translations.literal: Jiao, Kang, Di correspond to Yan province.
translations.idiomatic: Jiao, Kang, Di correspond to Yan province.

translations.literal: Fang and Xin correspond to Yu province.
translations.idiomatic: Fang and Xin correspond to Yu province.

translations.literal: Wei and Ji correspond to You province.
translations.idiomatic: Wei and Ji correspond to You province.

translations.literal: Dou corresponds to Jiang and Hu regions.
translations.idiomatic: Dou corresponds to Jiang and Hu regions.

translations.literal: Altair and Wu Maiden correspond to Yang province.
translations.idiomatic: Altair and Wu Maiden correspond to Yang province.

translations.literal: Xu and Wei correspond to Qing province.
translations.idiomatic: Xu and Wei correspond to Qing province.

translations.literal: Yingshi and East Wall correspond to Bing province.
translations.idiomatic: Yingshi and East Wall correspond to Bing province.

translations.literal: Kui, Lou, Wei correspond to Xu province.
translations.idiomatic: Kui, Lou, Wei correspond to Xu province.

translations.literal: Mao and Bi correspond to Ji province.
translations.idiomatic: Mao and Bi correspond to Ji province.

translations.literal: Beak-and-Talon and Shen correspond to Yi province.
translations.idiomatic: Beak-and-Talon and Shen correspond to Yi province.

translations.literal: Eastern Well and Carriage Ghosts correspond to Yong province.
translations.idiomatic: Eastern Well and Carriage Ghosts correspond to Yong province.

translations.literal: Liu, Qixing, Zhang correspond to the Three Rivers.
