translations.idiomatic: Liu, Qixing, Zhang correspond to the Three Rivers.

translations.literal: Yi and Zhen correspond to Jing province.
translations.idiomatic: Yi and Zhen correspond to Jing province.

translations.literal: Jia and Yi are overseas; sun and moon are not used for those divinations.
translations.idiomatic: Jia and Yi are overseas; sun and moon are not used for those divinations.

translations.literal: Bing and Ding correspond to Jiang, Huai, sea, and Tai regions.
translations.idiomatic: Bing and Ding correspond to Jiang, Huai, sea, and Tai regions.

translations.literal: Wu and Ji correspond to central plains between Yellow and Ji rivers.
translations.idiomatic: Wu and Ji correspond to central plains between Yellow and Ji rivers.

translations.literal: Geng and Xin correspond to west of Mount Hua.
translations.idiomatic: Geng and Xin correspond to west of Mount Hua.

translations.literal: Ren and Gui correspond to north of Mount Chang.
translations.idiomatic: Ren and Gui correspond to north of Mount Chang.

sentences.zh: 一曰，甲齊，乙東夷，丙楚，丁南夷，戊魏，己韓，庚秦，辛西夷，壬燕、趙，癸北夷。

translations.literal: Another scheme: Jia=Qi, Yi=eastern barbarians, Bing=Chu, Ding=southern barbarians, Wu=Wei, Ji=Han, Geng=Qin, Xin=western barbarians, Ren=Yan and Zhao, Gui=northern barbarians.
translations.idiomatic: Another scheme: Jia=Qi, Yi=eastern barbarians, Bing=Chu, Ding=southern barbarians, Wu=Wei, Ji=Han, Geng=Qin, Xin=western barbarians, Ren=Yan and Zhao, Gui=northern barbarians.

sentences.zh: 子周，丑翟，寅趙，卯鄭，辰邯鄲，巳衛，午秦，未中山，申齊，酉魯，戌吳、越，亥燕、代。

translations.literal: Zi=Zhou, Chou=Di, Yin=Zhao, Mao=Zheng, Chen=Handan, Si=Wei, Wu=Qin, Wei=Zhongshan, Shen=Qi, You=Lu, Xu=Wu and Yue, Hai=Yan and Dai.
translations.idiomatic: Zi=Zhou, Chou=Di, Yin=Zhao, Mao=Zheng, Chen=Handan, Si=Wei, Wu=Qin, Wei=Zhongshan, Shen=Qi, You=Lu, Xu=Wu and Yue, Hai=Yan and Dai.

translations.literal: At Qin's frontier, watch Venus and divine from Wolf and Bow.
translations.idiomatic: At Qin's frontier, watch Venus and divine from Wolf and Bow.

sentences.zh: 吳、楚之疆，候熒惑，占鳥衡。

translations.literal: At Wu and Chu frontiers, watch Mars and divine from Bird-Balance.
translations.idiomatic: At Wu and Chu frontiers, watch Mars and divine from Bird-Balance.

sentences.zh: 燕、齊之疆，候辰星，占虛、危。

translations.literal: At Yan and Qi frontiers, watch Mercury and divine from Xu and Wei.
translations.idiomatic: At Yan and Qi frontiers, watch Mercury and divine from Xu and Wei.

sentences.zh: 宋、鄭之疆，候歲星，占房、心。

translations.literal: At Song and Zheng frontiers, watch Jupiter and divine from Fang and Xin.
translations.idiomatic: At Song and Zheng frontiers, watch Jupiter and divine from Fang and Xin.

sentences.zh: 晉之疆，亦候辰星，占參、罰。

translations.literal: At Jin frontier, also watch Mercury and divine from Shen and Punishment.
translations.idiomatic: At Jin frontier, also watch Mercury and divine from Shen and Punishment.

sentences.zh: 及秦并吞三晉、燕、代，自河、山以南者中國。

translations.literal: After Qin swallowed the three Jin and Yan-Dai, lands south of Yellow River and mountains were the central states.
translations.idiomatic: After Qin swallowed the three Jin and Yan-Dai, lands south of Yellow River and mountains were the central states.

sentences.zh: 中國於四海內則在東南，為陽，陽則日、歲星、熒惑、填星，占於街南，畢主之。

translations.literal: Within the four seas, the central states lie to southeast and are yang; thus sun, Jupiter, Mars, and Saturn are divined at south of Heavenly Street, governed by Bi.
translations.idiomatic: Within the four seas, the central states lie to southeast and are yang; thus sun, Jupiter, Mars, and Saturn are divined at south of Heavenly Street, governed by Bi.

sentences.zh: 其西北則胡、貉、月氏旃裘引弓之民，為陰，陰則月、太白、辰星，占於街北，昴主之。

translations.literal: To northwest are Hu, He, Yuezhi, and fur-clad bow-drawing peoples, as yin; thus moon, Venus, and Mercury are divined north of Heavenly Street, governed by Mao.
translations.idiomatic: To northwest are Hu, He, Yuezhi, and fur-clad bow-drawing peoples, as yin; thus moon, Venus, and Mercury are divined north of Heavenly Street, governed by Mao.

sentences.zh: 故中國山川東北流，其維，首在隴、蜀，尾沒於勃海碣石。

translations.literal: Therefore rivers of the central states flow northeastward; their main cord begins in Long and Shu and ends submerged at Jieshi in Bohai.
translations.idiomatic: Therefore rivers of the central states flow northeastward; their main cord begins in Long and Shu and ends submerged at Jieshi in Bohai.

sentences.zh: 是以秦、晉好用兵，復占太白。

translations.literal: Hence Qin and Jin favored military use and repeatedly divined by Venus.
translations.idiomatic: Hence Qin and Jin favored military use and repeatedly divined by Venus.

sentences.zh: 太白主中國，而胡、貉數侵掠，獨占辰星。

translations.literal: Venus governs the central states, while Hu and He repeatedly raided, so they uniquely divined by Mercury.
translations.idiomatic: Venus governs the central states, while Hu and He repeatedly raided, so they uniquely divined by Mercury.

sentences.zh: 辰星出入趮疾，常主夷狄，其大經也。

translations.literal: Mercury's rapid entries and exits usually govern the barbarians; this is its major principle.
translations.idiomatic: Mercury's rapid entries and exits usually govern the barbarians; this is its major principle.

sentences.zh: 凡五星，早出為贏，贏為客；

translations.literal: For five planets in general, early emergence is advance, and advance is guest.
translations.idiomatic: For five planets in general, early emergence is advance, and advance is guest.

translations.literal: Late emergence is contraction, and contraction is host.
translations.idiomatic: Late emergence is contraction, and contraction is host.

sentences.zh: 五星贏縮，必有天應見杓。

translations.literal: With advance and contraction of the five planets, a heavenly response must appear at the Dipper handle.
translations.idiomatic: With advance and contraction of the five planets, a heavenly response must appear at the Dipper handle.

translations.literal: When the Great Year is in Yin, it is called Sheti-ge.
translations.idiomatic: When the Great Year is in Yin, it is called Sheti-ge.

sentences.zh: 歲星正月晨出東方，石氏曰名監德，在斗、牽牛。

translations.literal: When Jupiter emerges at dawn in first month eastward, Shi school names it Jiande, in Dou and Altair.
translations.idiomatic: When Jupiter emerges at dawn in first month eastward, Shi school names it Jiande, in Dou and Altair.

translations.literal: If out of sequence: at handle, early floods, late droughts.
translations.idiomatic: If out of sequence: at handle, early floods, late droughts.

translations.literal: Gan school places it in Jian and Wu Maiden.
translations.idiomatic: Gan school places it in Jian and Wu Maiden.

translations.literal: Taichu calendar places it in Yingshi and East Wall.
translations.idiomatic: Taichu calendar places it in Yingshi and East Wall.

translations.literal: When in Mao it is called Dan'e.
translations.idiomatic: When in Mao it is called Dan'e.

sentences.zh: 二月出，石氏曰名降入，在婺女、虛、危。

translations.literal: Emerging in second month, Shi school names it Jiangru, in Wu Maiden, Xu, and Wei.
translations.idiomatic: Emerging in second month, Shi school names it Jiangru, in Wu Maiden, Xu, and Wei.

translations.literal: Gan school places it in Xu and Wei.
translations.idiomatic: Gan school places it in Xu and Wei.

translations.literal: If out of sequence: at handle, there is flood disaster.
translations.idiomatic: If out of sequence: at handle, there is flood disaster.

translations.literal: Taichu places it in Kui and Lou.
translations.idiomatic: Taichu places it in Kui and Lou.

translations.literal: When in Chen it is called Zhixu.
translations.idiomatic: When in Chen it is called Zhixu.

sentences.zh: 三月出，石氏曰名青章，在營室、東壁。

translations.literal: Emerging in third month, Shi school names it Qingzhang, in Yingshi and East Wall.
translations.idiomatic: Emerging in third month, Shi school names it Qingzhang, in Yingshi and East Wall.

translations.literal: If out of sequence: at handle, early drought and late flood.
translations.idiomatic: If out of sequence: at handle, early drought and late flood.

translations.literal: Gan school agrees.
translations.idiomatic: Gan school agrees.

translations.literal: Taichu places it in Wei and Mao.
translations.idiomatic: Taichu places it in Wei and Mao.

translations.literal: When in Si it is called Dahuangluo.
translations.idiomatic: When in Si it is called Dahuangluo.

sentences.zh: 四月出，石氏曰名路踵，在奎、婁。

translations.literal: Emerging in fourth month, Shi school names it Luzhong, in Kui and Lou.
translations.idiomatic: Emerging in fourth month, Shi school names it Luzhong, in Kui and Lou.

translations.literal: Gan school agrees.
translations.idiomatic: Gan school agrees.

translations.literal: Taichu places it in Shen and Punishment.
translations.idiomatic: Taichu places it in Shen and Punishment.

translations.literal: When in Wu it is called Dunzang.
translations.idiomatic: When in Wu it is called Dunzang.

sentences.zh: 五月出，石氏曰名啟明，在胃、昴、畢。

translations.literal: Emerging in fifth month, Shi school names it Qiming, in Wei, Mao, and Bi.
translations.idiomatic: Emerging in fifth month, Shi school names it Qiming, in Wei, Mao, and Bi.

translations.literal: If out of sequence: at handle, early drought and late flood.
translations.idiomatic: If out of sequence: at handle, early drought and late flood.

translations.literal: Gan school agrees.
translations.idiomatic: Gan school agrees.

translations.literal: Taichu places it in Eastern Well and Carriage Ghosts.
translations.idiomatic: Taichu places it in Eastern Well and Carriage Ghosts.

translations.literal: When in Wei it is called Xieqia.
translations.idiomatic: When in Wei it is called Xieqia.

sentences.zh: 六月出，石氏曰名長烈，在觜觿、參。

translations.literal: Emerging in sixth month, Shi school names it Changlie, in Beak-and-Talon and Shen.
translations.idiomatic: Emerging in sixth month, Shi school names it Changlie, in Beak-and-Talon and Shen.

translations.literal: Gan school places it in Shen and Punishment.
translations.idiomatic: Gan school places it in Shen and Punishment.

translations.literal: Taichu places it in Yi, Zhang, and Qixing.
translations.idiomatic: Taichu places it in Yi, Zhang, and Qixing.

translations.literal: When in Shen it is called Tuntan.
translations.idiomatic: When in Shen it is called Tuntan.

translations.literal: It emerges in seventh month.
translations.idiomatic: It emerges in seventh month.

sentences.zh: 石氏曰名天晉，在東井、輿鬼。

translations.literal: Shi school names it Tianjin, in Eastern Well and Carriage Ghosts.
translations.idiomatic: Shi school names it Tianjin, in Eastern Well and Carriage Ghosts.

translations.literal: Gan school places it in Bow.
translations.idiomatic: Gan school places it in Bow.

translations.literal: Taichu places it in Yi and Zhen.
translations.idiomatic: Taichu places it in Yi and Zhen.

translations.literal: When in You it is called Zuoe.
translations.idiomatic: When in You it is called Zuoe.

translations.literal: Erya writes it as Zuo'e.
translations.idiomatic: Erya writes it as Zuo'e.

sentences.zh: 八月出，石氏曰名長壬，在柳、七星、張。

translations.literal: Emerging in eighth month, Shi school names it Changren, in Liu, Qixing, and Zhang.
translations.idiomatic: Emerging in eighth month, Shi school names it Changren, in Liu, Qixing, and Zhang.

translations.literal: If out of sequence: at Dipper handle there is mourning of women and illness among people.
translations.idiomatic: If out of sequence: at Dipper handle there is mourning of women and illness among people.

translations.literal: Gan school places it in Yi and Zhang.
translations.idiomatic: Gan school places it in Yi and Zhang.

translations.literal: If out of sequence: at handle there is fire.
translations.idiomatic: If out of sequence: at handle there is fire.

translations.literal: Taichu places it in Jiao and Kang.
translations.idiomatic: Taichu places it in Jiao and Kang.

translations.literal: When in Xu it is called Yanmao.
translations.idiomatic: When in Xu it is called Yanmao.

sentences.zh: 九月出，石氏曰名天睢，在翼、軫。

translations.literal: Emerging in ninth month, Shi school names it Tiansui, in Yi and Zhen.
translations.idiomatic: Emerging in ninth month, Shi school names it Tiansui, in Yi and Zhen.

translations.literal: If out of sequence: at handle there is flood.
translations.idiomatic: If out of sequence: at handle there is flood.

translations.literal: Gan school places it in Qixing and Yi.
translations.idiomatic: Gan school places it in Qixing and Yi.

translations.literal: Taichu places it in Di, Fang, and Xin.
translations.idiomatic: Taichu places it in Di, Fang, and Xin.

translations.literal: When in Hai it is called Dayuanxian.
translations.idiomatic: When in Hai it is called Dayuanxian.

sentences.zh: 十月出，石氏曰名天皇，在角、亢始。

translations.literal: Emerging in tenth month, Shi school names it Tianhuang, beginning in Jiao and Kang.
translations.idiomatic: Emerging in tenth month, Shi school names it Tianhuang, beginning in Jiao and Kang.

translations.literal: Gan school places it in Zhen, Jiao, and Kang.
translations.idiomatic: Gan school places it in Zhen, Jiao, and Kang.

translations.literal: Taichu places it in Wei and Ji.
translations.idiomatic: Taichu places it in Wei and Ji.

translations.literal: When in Zi it is called Kundun.
translations.idiomatic: When in Zi it is called Kundun.

sentences.zh: 十一月出，石氏曰名天宗，在氐、房始。

translations.literal: Emerging in eleventh month, Shi school names it Tianzong, beginning in Di and Fang.
translations.idiomatic: Emerging in eleventh month, Shi school names it Tianzong, beginning in Di and Fang.

translations.literal: Gan school is the same.
translations.idiomatic: Gan school is the same.

translations.literal: Taichu places it in Jian star and Altair.
translations.idiomatic: Taichu places it in Jian star and Altair.

translations.literal: When in Chou it is called Chifenruo.
translations.idiomatic: When in Chou it is called Chifenruo.

sentences.zh: 十二月出，石氏曰名天昊，在尾、箕。

translations.literal: Emerging in twelfth month, Shi school names it Tianhao, in Wei and Ji.
translations.idiomatic: Emerging in twelfth month, Shi school names it Tianhao, in Wei and Ji.

translations.literal: Gan school places it in Xin and Wei.
translations.idiomatic: Gan school places it in Xin and Wei.

translations.literal: Taichu places it in Wu Maiden, Xu, and Wei.
translations.idiomatic: Taichu places it in Wu Maiden, Xu, and Wei.

sentences.zh: 甘氏、太初曆所以不同者，以星贏縮在前，各錄後所見也。

translations.literal: Why Gan school and Taichu calendar differ is that planetary advance and contraction are recorded ahead of time, each recording what was later observed.
translations.idiomatic: Why Gan school and Taichu calendar differ is that planetary advance and contraction are recorded ahead of time, each recording what was later observed.

translations.literal: The other four planets are roughly similar in this respect.
translations.idiomatic: The other four planets are roughly similar in this respect.

sentences.zh: 古曆五星之推，亡逆行者，至甘氏、石氏經，以熒惑、太白為有逆行。

translations.literal: In ancient calendrical methods for five planets, there was no retrograde motion; by the canons of Gan and Shi schools, Mars and Venus are treated as retrograde.
translations.idiomatic: In ancient calendrical methods for five planets, there was no retrograde motion; by the canons of Gan and Shi schools, Mars and Venus are treated as retrograde.

translations.literal: Now calendrics concerns proper motion.
translations.idiomatic: Now calendrics concerns proper motion.

sentences.zh: 古人有言曰：「天下太平，五星循度，亡有逆行。

translations.literal: An ancient saying says: "When all under heaven is at peace, the five planets follow their degrees, with no retrograde motion.
translations.idiomatic: An ancient saying says: "When all under heaven is at peace, the five planets follow their degrees, with no retrograde motion.

translations.literal: The sun does not eclipse at new moon, the moon does not eclipse at full moon."
translations.idiomatic: The sun does not eclipse at new moon, the moon does not eclipse at full moon."

sentences.zh: 夏氏日月傳曰：「日月食盡，主位也；

translations.literal: Xia school Sun-Moon tradition says: "If eclipse is total, it is ruler's station.
translations.idiomatic: Xia school Sun-Moon tradition says: "If eclipse is total, it is ruler's station.

translations.literal: If not total, it is minister's station."
translations.idiomatic: If not total, it is minister's station."

sentences.zh: 星傳曰：「日者德也，月者刑也，故曰日食修德，月食修刑。」

translations.literal: Star traditions say: "Sun is virtue, moon is punishment; therefore with solar eclipse one cultivates virtue, with lunar eclipse one rectifies punishments."
translations.idiomatic: Star traditions say: "Sun is virtue, moon is punishment; therefore with solar eclipse one cultivates virtue, with lunar eclipse one rectifies punishments."

sentences.zh: 然而曆紀推月食，與二星之逆亡異。

translations.literal: Yet when calendrical records compute lunar eclipses, they are not different from the retrograde behavior of these two planets.
translations.idiomatic: Yet when calendrical records compute lunar eclipses, they are not different from the retrograde behavior of these two planets.

sentences.zh: 熒惑主內亂，太白主兵，月主刑。

translations.literal: Mars governs internal disorder, Venus governs war, moon governs punishment.
translations.idiomatic: Mars governs internal disorder, Venus governs war, moon governs punishment.

sentences.zh: 自周室衰，亂臣賊子師旅數起，刑罰失中，雖其亡亂臣賊子師旅之變，內臣猶不治，四夷猶不服，兵革猶不寢，刑罰猶不錯，故二星與月為之失度，三變常見；

translations.literal: Since Zhou house declined, rebellious ministers, traitorous sons, and armies repeatedly arose; punishments lost balance. Even when there were no such upheavals, inner ministers were still ungoverned, border peoples unsubdued, warfare unceasing, punishments uncorrected. Therefore the two planets and moon lost proper measure, and these three changes were commonly seen.
translations.idiomatic: Since Zhou house declined, rebellious ministers, traitorous sons, and armies repeatedly arose; punishments lost balance. Even when there were no such upheavals, inner ministers were still ungoverned, border peoples unsubdued, warfare unceasing, punishments uncorrected. Therefore the two planets and moon lost proper measure, and these three changes were commonly seen.

sentences.zh: 及有亂臣賊子伏尸流血之兵，大變乃出。

translations.literal: When there are rebellious ministers and bloody warfare with corpses, then great anomalies appear.
translations.idiomatic: When there are rebellious ministers and bloody warfare with corpses, then great anomalies appear.

sentences.zh: 甘、石氏見其常然，因以為紀，皆非正行也。

translations.literal: Gan and Shi schools saw this as common and took it as rule, yet none of it is proper motion.
translations.idiomatic: Gan and Shi schools saw this as common and took it as rule, yet none of it is proper motion.

translations.literal: The Odes says: "That moon being eclipsed - that is ordinary enough.
translations.idiomatic: The Odes says: "That moon being eclipsed - that is ordinary enough.

translations.literal: But this sun being eclipsed - how is this not disastrous?"
translations.idiomatic: But this sun being eclipsed - how is this not disastrous?"

sentences.zh: 詩傳曰：「月食非常也，比之日食猶常也，日食則不臧矣。」

translations.literal: The ode tradition says: "Lunar eclipse is not ordinary, yet compared with solar eclipse it is still relatively ordinary; solar eclipse is truly harmful."
translations.idiomatic: The ode tradition says: "Lunar eclipse is not ordinary, yet compared with solar eclipse it is still relatively ordinary; solar eclipse is truly harmful."

translations.literal: Calling it a minor anomaly is acceptable.
translations.idiomatic: Calling it a minor anomaly is acceptable.

translations.literal: Calling it proper motion is not.
translations.idiomatic: Calling it proper motion is not.

sentences.zh: 故熒惑必行十六舍，去日遠而顓恣。

translations.literal: Thus Mars must travel sixteen lodges, and when far from sun becomes arbitrary.
translations.idiomatic: Thus Mars must travel sixteen lodges, and when far from sun becomes arbitrary.

sentences.zh: 太白出西方，進在日前，氣盛乃逆行。

translations.literal: Venus emerging in west advances ahead of sun; when qi is strong it then retrogrades.
translations.idiomatic: Venus emerging in west advances ahead of sun; when qi is strong it then retrogrades.

sentences.zh: 及月必食於望，亦誅盛也。

translations.literal: And the moon must eclipse at full moon; this too is punishing excess.
translations.idiomatic: And the moon must eclipse at full moon; this too is punishing excess.

sentences.zh: 國皇星，大而赤，狀類南極。

translations.literal: State-Sovereign Star is large and red, resembling Southern Pole.
translations.idiomatic: State-Sovereign Star is large and red, resembling Southern Pole.

translations.literal: Where it appears, troops rise below.
translations.idiomatic: Where it appears, troops rise below.

translations.literal: If troops are strong, colliding against it is unfavorable.
translations.idiomatic: If troops are strong, colliding against it is unfavorable.

sentences.zh: 昭明星，大而白，無角，乍上乍下。

translations.literal: Radiant Star is large and white, without horns, rising and sinking abruptly.
translations.idiomatic: Radiant Star is large and white, without horns, rising and sinking abruptly.

translations.literal: In the state where it appears, troops rise and many changes occur.
translations.idiomatic: In the state where it appears, troops rise and many changes occur.

sentences.zh: 五殘星，出正東，東方之星。

translations.literal: Five-Remnant Star emerges due east, an eastern star.
translations.idiomatic: Five-Remnant Star emerges due east, an eastern star.

sentences.zh: 其狀類辰，去地可六丈，大而黃。

translations.literal: Its form resembles Mercury, about six zhang above ground, large and yellow.
translations.idiomatic: Its form resembles Mercury, about six zhang above ground, large and yellow.

sentences.zh: 六賊星，出正南，南方之星。

translations.literal: Six-Bandit Star emerges due south, a southern star.
translations.idiomatic: Six-Bandit Star emerges due south, a southern star.

sentences.zh: 去地可六丈，大而赤，數動，有光。

translations.literal: About six zhang above ground, large and red, frequently moving, with light.
translations.idiomatic: About six zhang above ground, large and red, frequently moving, with light.

sentences.zh: 司詭星，出正西，西方之星。

translations.literal: Controller-of-Deceit Star emerges due west, a western star.
translations.idiomatic: Controller-of-Deceit Star emerges due west, a western star.

sentences.zh: 去地可六丈，大而白，類太白。

translations.literal: About six zhang above ground, large and white, resembling Venus.
translations.idiomatic: About six zhang above ground, large and white, resembling Venus.

sentences.zh: 咸漢星，出正北，北方之星。

translations.literal: Salty-River Star emerges due north, a northern star.
translations.idiomatic: Salty-River Star emerges due north, a northern star.

sentences.zh: 去地可六丈，大而赤，數動，察之中青。

translations.literal: About six zhang above ground, large and red, moving frequently; on close inspection green at center.
translations.idiomatic: About six zhang above ground, large and red, moving frequently; on close inspection green at center.

sentences.zh: 此四星所出非其方，其下有兵，衝不利。

translations.literal: If these four stars emerge outside their proper directions, there is war below; collision with them is unfavorable.
translations.idiomatic: If these four stars emerge outside their proper directions, there is war below; collision with them is unfavorable.

sentences.zh: 四填星，出四隅，去地可四丈。

translations.literal: Four-Fill Stars emerge from the four corners, about four zhang above ground.
translations.idiomatic: Four-Fill Stars emerge from the four corners, about four zhang above ground.

sentences.zh: 地維臧光，亦出四隅，去地可二丈，若月始出。

translations.literal: Earth-Cord Hidden-Light also emerges from four corners, about two zhang above ground, like the moon's first rise.
translations.idiomatic: Earth-Cord Hidden-Light also emerges from four corners, about two zhang above ground, like the moon's first rise.

sentences.zh: 所見下，有亂者亡，有德者昌。

translations.literal: Where they are seen, the disorderly perish and the virtuous prosper.
translations.idiomatic: Where they are seen, the disorderly perish and the virtuous prosper.

sentences.zh: 燭星，狀如太白，其出也不行，見則滅。

translations.literal: Torch Star resembles Venus; when it appears it does not move, and vanishes once seen.
translations.idiomatic: Torch Star resembles Venus; when it appears it does not move, and vanishes once seen.

translations.literal: Where it shines, city-states fall into disorder.
translations.idiomatic: Where it shines, city-states fall into disorder.

sentences.zh: 如星非星，如雲非雲，名曰歸邪。

translations.literal: Something like a star but not a star, like cloud but not cloud, is called Returning Evil.
translations.idiomatic: Something like a star but not a star, like cloud but not cloud, is called Returning Evil.

translations.literal: When Returning Evil appears, there must be those returning to their state.
translations.idiomatic: When Returning Evil appears, there must be those returning to their state.

sentences.zh: 星者，金之散氣，其本曰人。

translations.literal: Stars are scattered qi of metal; their root corresponds to humans.
translations.idiomatic: Stars are scattered qi of metal; their root corresponds to humans.

translations.literal: Many stars: state auspicious; few: inauspicious.
translations.idiomatic: Many stars: state auspicious; few: inauspicious.

sentences.zh: 漢者，亦金散氣，其本曰水。

translations.literal: The Milky River is also scattered qi of metal; its root corresponds to water.
translations.idiomatic: The Milky River is also scattered qi of metal; its root corresponds to water.

sentences.zh: 星多，多水，少則旱，其大經也。

translations.literal: If many stars then much water; if few then drought - this is the main rule.
translations.idiomatic: If many stars then much water; if few then drought - this is the main rule.

sentences.zh: 天鼓，有音如雷非雷，音在地而下及地。

translations.literal: Heavenly Drum has a sound like thunder but not thunder; the sound is in the air yet falls to earth.
translations.idiomatic: Heavenly Drum has a sound like thunder but not thunder; the sound is in the air yet falls to earth.

translations.literal: Where it halts, armies arise below.
translations.idiomatic: Where it halts, armies arise below.

sentences.zh: 天狗，狀如大流星，有聲，共下止地，類狗。

translations.literal: Heavenly Dog resembles a great shooting star, with sound, falling together and stopping on ground, shaped like a dog.
translations.idiomatic: Heavenly Dog resembles a great shooting star, with sound, falling together and stopping on ground, shaped like a dog.

sentences.zh: 所墜及，望之如火光炎炎中天。

translations.literal: Where it falls, viewed from afar it blazes like fire in mid-sky.
translations.idiomatic: Where it falls, viewed from afar it blazes like fire in mid-sky.

sentences.zh: 其下圜如數頃田處，上銳見則有黃色，千里破軍殺將。

translations.literal: Its lower part is round like several qing of farmland, upper part sharp; when seen it has yellow color, and within a thousand li armies are broken and generals killed.
translations.idiomatic: Its lower part is round like several qing of farmland, upper part sharp; when seen it has yellow color, and within a thousand li armies are broken and generals killed.

sentences.zh: 格澤者，如炎火之狀，黃白，起地而上，下大上銳。

translations.literal: Gezhe appears like blazing fire, yellow-white, rising from ground upward, broad below and sharp above.
translations.idiomatic: Gezhe appears like blazing fire, yellow-white, rising from ground upward, broad below and sharp above.

translations.literal: When it appears, crops are reaped without sowing.
translations.idiomatic: When it appears, crops are reaped without sowing.

translations.literal: If there is no major earthwork, there will be a great visitor force.
translations.idiomatic: If there is no major earthwork, there will be a great visitor force.

sentences.zh: 蚩尤之旗，類彗而後曲，象旗。

translations.literal: Chiyou's Banner resembles a comet with curved rear, like a banner.
translations.idiomatic: Chiyou's Banner resembles a comet with curved rear, like a banner.

translations.literal: When seen, the ruler campaigns and subdues the four directions.
translations.idiomatic: When seen, the ruler campaigns and subdues the four directions.

sentences.zh: 旬始，出於北斗旁，狀如雄雞。

translations.literal: Xunshi emerges beside Northern Dipper, shaped like a rooster.
translations.idiomatic: Xunshi emerges beside Northern Dipper, shaped like a rooster.

translations.literal: When enraged it is blue-black, like a crouching turtle.
translations.idiomatic: When enraged it is blue-black, like a crouching turtle.

sentences.zh: 枉矢，狀類大流星，蛇行而倉黑，望如有毛目然。

translations.literal: Bent Arrow resembles a great shooting star, snakelike in motion and dark-black; seen from afar it seems to have hairy eyes.
translations.idiomatic: Bent Arrow resembles a great shooting star, snakelike in motion and dark-black; seen from afar it seems to have hairy eyes.

translations.literal: Long Geng is as broad as one bolt of cloth spread across sky.
translations.idiomatic: Long Geng is as broad as one bolt of cloth spread across sky.

translations.literal: When this star appears, armies rise.
translations.idiomatic: When this star appears, armies rise.

translations.literal: When star vapors condense to ground, they become stones.
translations.idiomatic: When star vapors condense to ground, they become stones.

translations.literal: When heaven is clear, a Beneficent Star appears.
translations.idiomatic: When heaven is clear, a Beneficent Star appears.

sentences.zh: 景星者，德星也，其狀無常，常出於有道之國。

translations.literal: Beneficent Star is a Virtue Star; its form is inconstant, and it regularly appears in states with the Way.
translations.idiomatic: Beneficent Star is a Virtue Star; its form is inconstant, and it regularly appears in states with the Way.

translations.literal: The sun has one central path; the moon has nine courses.
translations.idiomatic: The sun has one central path; the moon has nine courses.

translations.literal: The central path is the yellow path, also called the bright path.
translations.idiomatic: The central path is the yellow path, also called the bright path.

sentences.zh: 光道北至東井，去北極近；

translations.literal: The bright path reaches north to Eastern Well, closest to the North Pole.
translations.idiomatic: The bright path reaches north to Eastern Well, closest to the North Pole.

translations.literal: Southward it reaches Altair, far from North Pole.
translations.idiomatic: Southward it reaches Altair, far from North Pole.

translations.literal: Eastward to Horn, westward to Lou, midway from the Pole.
translations.idiomatic: Eastward to Horn, westward to Lou, midway from the Pole.

sentences.zh: 夏至至於東井，北近極，故晷短；

translations.literal: At summer solstice it reaches Eastern Well, near the Pole, therefore the gnomon shadow is short.
translations.idiomatic: At summer solstice it reaches Eastern Well, near the Pole, therefore the gnomon shadow is short.

sentences.zh: 立八尺之表，而晷景長尺五寸八分。

translations.literal: With an eight-chi gnomon, the shadow length is one chi five cun eight fen.
translations.idiomatic: With an eight-chi gnomon, the shadow length is one chi five cun eight fen.

sentences.zh: 冬至至於牽牛，遠極，故晷長；

translations.literal: At winter solstice it reaches Altair, far from the Pole, therefore the shadow is long.
translations.idiomatic: At winter solstice it reaches Altair, far from the Pole, therefore the shadow is long.

sentences.zh: 立八尺之表，而晷景長丈三尺一寸四分。

translations.literal: With an eight-chi gnomon, the shadow length is one zhang three chi one cun four fen.
translations.idiomatic: With an eight-chi gnomon, the shadow length is one zhang three chi one cun four fen.

sentences.zh: 春秋分日至婁、角，去極中，而晷中；

translations.literal: At spring and autumn equinox it reaches Lou and Horn, midway from Pole, and the shadow is medium.
translations.idiomatic: At spring and autumn equinox it reaches Lou and Horn, midway from Pole, and the shadow is medium.

sentences.zh: 立八尺之表，而晷景長七尺三寸六分。

translations.literal: With an eight-chi gnomon, the shadow length is seven chi three cun six fen.
translations.idiomatic: With an eight-chi gnomon, the shadow length is seven chi three cun six fen.

sentences.zh: 此日去極遠近之差，晷景長短之制也。

translations.literal: These are the differences in sun's distance from Pole and the standards of shadow length.
translations.idiomatic: These are the differences in sun's distance from Pole and the standards of shadow length.

sentences.zh: 去極遠近難知，要以晷景。

translations.literal: Distance from Pole is hard to know directly; one relies on shadow.
translations.idiomatic: Distance from Pole is hard to know directly; one relies on shadow.

sentences.zh: 晷景者，所以知日之南北也。

translations.literal: Shadow is how one knows whether the sun is north or south.
translations.idiomatic: Shadow is how one knows whether the sun is north or south.

translations.literal: The sun is yang.
translations.idiomatic: The sun is yang.

sentences.zh: 陽用事則日進而北，晝進而長，陽勝，故為溫暑；

translations.literal: When yang is in action, the sun advances north, daylight advances and lengthens; yang prevails, thus warmth and heat.
translations.idiomatic: When yang is in action, the sun advances north, daylight advances and lengthens; yang prevails, thus warmth and heat.

sentences.zh: 陰用事則日退而南，晝退而短，陰勝，故為涼寒也。

translations.literal: When yin is in action, the sun retreats south, daylight retreats and shortens; yin prevails, thus coolness and cold.
translations.idiomatic: When yin is in action, the sun retreats south, daylight retreats and shortens; yin prevails, thus coolness and cold.

translations.literal: Therefore sun's advance brings heat, retreat brings cold.
translations.idiomatic: Therefore sun's advance brings heat, retreat brings cold.

sentences.zh: 若日之南北失節，晷過而長為常寒，退而短為常燠。

translations.literal: If the sun's north-south rhythm loses season, over-advance with long shadow gives persistent cold; over-retreat with short shadow gives persistent heat.
translations.idiomatic: If the sun's north-south rhythm loses season, over-advance with long shadow gives persistent cold; over-retreat with short shadow gives persistent heat.

translations.literal: This is the indicator of cold and heat.
translations.idiomatic: This is the indicator of cold and heat.

translations.literal: Hence it is said to govern cold and heat.
translations.idiomatic: Hence it is said to govern cold and heat.

sentences.zh: 一曰，晷長為潦，短為旱，奢為扶。

translations.literal: One saying: long shadow means flood, short means drought, excess means support-faction.
translations.idiomatic: One saying: long shadow means flood, short means drought, excess means support-faction.

sentences.zh: 扶者，邪臣進而正臣疏，君子不足，姦人有餘。

translations.literal: Support-faction means crooked ministers advance and upright ministers are estranged, gentlemen insufficient and traitors in excess.
translations.idiomatic: Support-faction means crooked ministers advance and upright ministers are estranged, gentlemen insufficient and traitors in excess.

sentences.zh: 月有九行者：黑道二，出黃道北；

translations.literal: The moon has nine courses: two black paths, emerging north of yellow path.
translations.idiomatic: The moon has nine courses: two black paths, emerging north of yellow path.

translations.literal: Two red paths, emerging south of yellow path.
translations.idiomatic: Two red paths, emerging south of yellow path.

translations.literal: Two white paths, emerging west of yellow path.
translations.idiomatic: Two white paths, emerging west of yellow path.

translations.literal: Two blue paths, emerging east of yellow path.
translations.idiomatic: Two blue paths, emerging east of yellow path.

translations.literal: At start of spring and spring equinox, moon goes east along blue path.
translations.idiomatic: At start of spring and spring equinox, moon goes east along blue path.

translations.literal: At start of autumn and autumn equinox, west along white path.
translations.idiomatic: At start of autumn and autumn equinox, west along white path.

translations.literal: At start of winter and winter solstice, north along black path.
translations.idiomatic: At start of winter and winter solstice, north along black path.

translations.literal: At start of summer and summer solstice, south along red path.
translations.idiomatic: At start of summer and summer solstice, south along red path.

translations.literal: Yet in application, one decisive standard is the central course through Fang.
translations.idiomatic: Yet in application, one decisive standard is the central course through Fang.

sentences.zh: 青赤出陽道，白黑出陰道。

translations.literal: Blue and red emerge from yang route; white and black emerge from yin route.
translations.idiomatic: Blue and red emerge from yang route; white and black emerge from yin route.

sentences.zh: 若月失節度而妄行，出陽道則旱風，出陰道則陰雨。

translations.literal: If moon loses proper rhythm and moves erratically, emerging on yang route brings drought-wind; on yin route brings continuous rain.
translations.idiomatic: If moon loses proper rhythm and moves erratically, emerging on yang route brings drought-wind; on yin route brings continuous rain.

sentences.zh: 凡君行急則日行疾，君行緩則日行遲。

translations.literal: In general, if ruler acts hastily, sun moves swiftly; if ruler acts slowly, sun moves slowly.
translations.idiomatic: In general, if ruler acts hastily, sun moves swiftly; if ruler acts slowly, sun moves slowly.

sentences.zh: 日行不可指而知也，故以二至二分之星為候。

translations.literal: Sun's motion cannot be pointed out directly, so stars of two solstices and two equinoxes are used as markers.
translations.idiomatic: Sun's motion cannot be pointed out directly, so stars of two solstices and two equinoxes are used as markers.

translations.literal: Sun moves east, stars rotate west.
translations.idiomatic: Sun moves east, stars rotate west.

translations.literal: At winter solstice dusk, Kui at 8 degrees culminates.
translations.idiomatic: At winter solstice dusk, Kui at 8 degrees culminates.

translations.literal: At summer solstice, Di at 13 degrees culminates.
translations.idiomatic: At summer solstice, Di at 13 degrees culminates.

translations.literal: At spring equinox, Liu at 1 degree culminates.
translations.idiomatic: At spring equinox, Liu at 1 degree culminates.

sentences.zh: 秋分，牽牛三度七分中：此其正行也。

translations.literal: At autumn equinox, Altair at 3 degrees 7 fen culminates: this is proper motion.
translations.idiomatic: At autumn equinox, Altair at 3 degrees 7 fen culminates: this is proper motion.

sentences.zh: 日行疾，則星西轉疾，事勢然也。

translations.literal: If sun moves fast, stars rotate west fast; this is natural tendency.
translations.idiomatic: If sun moves fast, stars rotate west fast; this is natural tendency.

sentences.zh: 故過中則疾，君行急之感也；

translations.literal: So if it passes culmination, it is fast: response to ruler's haste.
translations.idiomatic: So if it passes culmination, it is fast: response to ruler's haste.

sentences.zh: 不及中則遲，君行緩之象也。

translations.literal: If it fails to reach culmination, it is slow: sign of ruler's slowness.
translations.idiomatic: If it fails to reach culmination, it is slow: sign of ruler's slowness.

translations.literal: For moon's motion, judge by new and full moons.
translations.idiomatic: For moon's motion, judge by new and full moons.

translations.literal: In winter sun is south, in summer north.
translations.idiomatic: In winter sun is south, in summer north.

sentences.zh: 冬至於牽牛，夏至於東井。

translations.literal: At winter solstice it is at Altair; at summer solstice at Eastern Well.
translations.idiomatic: At winter solstice it is at Altair; at summer solstice at Eastern Well.

sentences.zh: 日之所行為中道，月、五星皆隨之也。

translations.literal: The sun's route is central path, and moon and five planets all follow it.
translations.idiomatic: The sun's route is central path, and moon and five planets all follow it.

translations.literal: Ji star governs wind; it is a northeastern star.
translations.idiomatic: Ji star governs wind; it is a northeastern star.

sentences.zh: 東北地事，天位也，故易曰「東北喪朋」。

translations.literal: Northeastern terrestrial affairs correspond to heavenly position; therefore the Changes says "In northeast one loses companions."
translations.idiomatic: Northeastern terrestrial affairs correspond to heavenly position; therefore the Changes says "In northeast one loses companions."

translations.literal: And Xun in southeast is wind.
translations.idiomatic: And Xun in southeast is wind.

sentences.zh: 風，陽中之陰，大臣之象也，其星，軫也。

translations.literal: Wind is yin within yang, image of high ministers; its star is Zhen.
translations.idiomatic: Wind is yin within yang, image of high ministers; its star is Zhen.

sentences.zh: 月去中道，移而東北入箕，若東南入軫，則多風。

translations.literal: If moon leaves central path and shifts northeast into Ji, or southeast into Zhen, there is much wind.
translations.idiomatic: If moon leaves central path and shifts northeast into Ji, or southeast into Zhen, there is much wind.

translations.literal: The west corresponds to rain.
translations.idiomatic: The west corresponds to rain.

translations.literal: Rain is the position of lesser yin.
translations.idiomatic: Rain is the position of lesser yin.

sentences.zh: 月去中道，移而西入畢，則多雨。

translations.literal: If moon leaves central path and shifts west into Bi, there is much rain.
translations.idiomatic: If moon leaves central path and shifts west into Bi, there is much rain.

sentences.zh: 故詩云「月離于畢，俾滂沱矣」，言多雨也。

translations.literal: Therefore the Odes says, "When moon departs to Bi, then downpour follows," meaning heavy rain.
translations.idiomatic: Therefore the Odes says, "When moon departs to Bi, then downpour follows," meaning heavy rain.

sentences.zh: 星傳曰「月入畢則將相有以家犯罪者」，言陰盛也。

translations.literal: Star tradition says, "When moon enters Bi, among generals and chancellors some will commit crimes by household," meaning yin is excessive.
translations.idiomatic: Star tradition says, "When moon enters Bi, among generals and chancellors some will commit crimes by household," meaning yin is excessive.

sentences.zh: 書曰「星有好風，星有好雨，月之從星，則以風雨」，言失中道而東西也。

translations.literal: The Book says, "There are stars favoring wind, stars favoring rain; when moon follows stars, then by wind and rain," meaning it loses central path and shifts east or west.
translations.idiomatic: The Book says, "There are stars favoring wind, stars favoring rain; when moon follows stars, then by wind and rain," meaning it loses central path and shifts east or west.

sentences.zh: 故星傳曰「月南入牽牛南戒，民間疾疫；

translations.literal: Therefore star tradition says, "When moon enters south of Altair, beware: among people there are epidemics.
translations.idiomatic: Therefore star tradition says, "When moon enters south of Altair, beware: among people there are epidemics.

sentences.zh: 月北入太微，出坐北，若犯坐，則下人謀上。」

translations.literal: When moon enters Taiwei and exits from the northern seat, or encroaches the seat, those below plot against those above."
translations.idiomatic: When moon enters Taiwei and exits from the northern seat, or encroaches the seat, those below plot against those above."

sentences.zh: 一曰月為風雨，日為寒溫。

translations.literal: One saying: moon governs wind and rain, sun governs cold and warmth.
translations.idiomatic: One saying: moon governs wind and rain, sun governs cold and warmth.

sentences.zh: 冬至日南極，晷長，南不極則溫為害；

translations.literal: At winter solstice sun is at southern extreme and shadow long; if not reaching the extreme south, excessive warmth causes harm.
translations.idiomatic: At winter solstice sun is at southern extreme and shadow long; if not reaching the extreme south, excessive warmth causes harm.

sentences.zh: 夏至日北極，晷短，北不極則寒為害。

translations.literal: At summer solstice sun is at northern extreme and shadow short; if not reaching extreme north, excessive cold causes harm.
translations.idiomatic: At summer solstice sun is at northern extreme and shadow short; if not reaching extreme north, excessive cold causes harm.

sentences.zh: 故書曰「日月之行，則有冬有夏」也。

translations.literal: Therefore the Book says, "In the movement of sun and moon, there is winter and summer."
translations.idiomatic: Therefore the Book says, "In the movement of sun and moon, there is winter and summer."

sentences.zh: 政治變於下，日月運於上矣。

translations.literal: When governance changes below, sun and moon motions change above.
translations.idiomatic: When governance changes below, sun and moon motions change above.

sentences.zh: 日出房北，為雨為陰，為亂為兵；

translations.literal: If sun rises north of Fang, there is rain and overcast, disorder and war.
translations.idiomatic: If sun rises north of Fang, there is rain and overcast, disorder and war.

translations.literal: If it rises south of Fang, there is drought and untimely death.
translations.idiomatic: If it rises south of Fang, there is drought and untimely death.

sentences.zh: 水旱至衝而應，及五星之變，必然之效也。

translations.literal: Flood and drought strike and respond, and with changes of five planets effects are inevitable.
translations.idiomatic: Flood and drought strike and respond, and with changes of five planets effects are inevitable.

translations.literal: When two armies face each other, if solar halos are equal, strength is equal.
translations.idiomatic: When two armies face each other, if solar halos are equal, strength is equal.

translations.literal: If thick, long, and large, there is a winner.
translations.idiomatic: If thick, long, and large, there is a winner.

translations.literal: If thin, short, and small, there is no clear winner.
translations.idiomatic: If thin, short, and small, there is no clear winner.

translations.literal: Multiple enclosing halos mean great defeat and destruction.
translations.idiomatic: Multiple enclosing halos mean great defeat and destruction.

sentences.zh: 抱為和，背為不和，為分離相去。

translations.literal: Encircling means harmony; backing means disharmony, separation and distancing.
translations.idiomatic: Encircling means harmony; backing means disharmony, separation and distancing.

sentences.zh: 直為自立，立兵破軍，若曰殺將。

translations.literal: A straight halo means self-assertion: armies are broken or generals killed.
translations.idiomatic: A straight halo means self-assertion: armies are broken or generals killed.

translations.literal: Encircling and covering means joy.
translations.idiomatic: Encircling and covering means joy.

translations.literal: If the ring is in the center, center side wins.
translations.idiomatic: If the ring is in the center, center side wins.

translations.literal: If outside, outer side wins.
translations.idiomatic: If outside, outer side wins.

translations.literal: Blue outside and red inside means parties withdraw in harmony.
translations.idiomatic: Blue outside and red inside means parties withdraw in harmony.

translations.literal: Red outside and blue inside means parties withdraw in hostility.
translations.idiomatic: Red outside and blue inside means parties withdraw in hostility.

sentences.zh: 氣暈先至而後去，居軍勝。

translations.literal: If vapor-halo arrives first and departs later, the army that holds position wins.
translations.idiomatic: If vapor-halo arrives first and departs later, the army that holds position wins.

sentences.zh: 先至先去，前有利，後有病；

translations.literal: If it arrives first and departs first, the front gains advantage, rear suffers.
translations.idiomatic: If it arrives first and departs first, the front gains advantage, rear suffers.

translations.literal: If it arrives later and departs later, front suffers and rear gains.
translations.idiomatic: If it arrives later and departs later, front suffers and rear gains.

sentences.zh: 後至先去，前後皆病，居軍不勝。

translations.literal: If it arrives later and departs first, both front and rear suffer, and holding army does not win.
translations.idiomatic: If it arrives later and departs first, both front and rear suffer, and holding army does not win.

sentences.zh: 見而去，其後發疾，雖勝亡功。

translations.literal: If seen then gone, disease breaks out afterward; even if victorious, no merit.
translations.idiomatic: If seen then gone, disease breaks out afterward; even if victorious, no merit.

translations.literal: If seen for more than half a day, merit is great.
translations.idiomatic: If seen for more than half a day, merit is great.

sentences.zh: 白鸶屈短，上下銳，有者下大流血。

translations.literal: White rainbow bent and short, sharp above and below: where it appears below, there is major bloodshed.
translations.idiomatic: White rainbow bent and short, sharp above and below: where it appears below, there is major bloodshed.

sentences.zh: 日暈制勝，近期三十日，遠期六十日。

translations.literal: Solar-halo victory signs apply near term thirty days, far term sixty days.
translations.idiomatic: Solar-halo victory signs apply near term thirty days, far term sixty days.

translations.literal: Its eclipse: what is eclipsed is disadvantaged.
translations.idiomatic: Its eclipse: what is eclipsed is disadvantaged.

translations.literal: Its reemergence: what reemerges is advantaged.
translations.idiomatic: Its reemergence: what reemerges is advantaged.

translations.literal: Otherwise, total eclipse signifies the host position.
translations.idiomatic: Otherwise, total eclipse signifies the host position.

sentences.zh: 以其直及日所躔加日時，用名其國。

translations.literal: Using where it points and sun's current lodge, plus day and hour, one names the state concerned.
translations.idiomatic: Using where it points and sun's current lodge, plus day and hour, one names the state concerned.

sentences.zh: 凡望雲氣，仰而望之，三四百里；

translations.literal: In observing cloud-qi, look upward: three to four hundred li.
translations.idiomatic: In observing cloud-qi, look upward: three to four hundred li.

sentences.zh: 平望，在桑榆上，千餘里，二千里；

translations.literal: On level view near mulberry-elm height: over one thousand to two thousand li.
translations.idiomatic: On level view near mulberry-elm height: over one thousand to two thousand li.

sentences.zh: 登高而望之，下屬地者居三千里。

translations.literal: From high elevation, what extends down to ground reaches three thousand li.
translations.idiomatic: From high elevation, what extends down to ground reaches three thousand li.

translations.literal: In cloud-qi warfare, the side above wins.
translations.idiomatic: In cloud-qi warfare, the side above wins.

translations.literal: South of Mount Hua, qi is black below and red above.
translations.idiomatic: South of Mount Hua, qi is black below and red above.

translations.literal: In Songgao and Three Rivers suburbs, qi is fully red.
translations.idiomatic: In Songgao and Three Rivers suburbs, qi is fully red.

translations.literal: North of Mount Chang, qi is black below and blue above.
translations.idiomatic: North of Mount Chang, qi is black below and blue above.

translations.literal: Between Bo, Jieshi, sea, and Tai, qi is all black.
translations.idiomatic: Between Bo, Jieshi, sea, and Tai, qi is all black.

translations.literal: Between Jiang and Huai, qi is all white.
translations.idiomatic: Between Jiang and Huai, qi is all white.

translations.literal: Infantry qi is white.
translations.idiomatic: Infantry qi is white.

translations.literal: Earthwork qi is yellow.
translations.idiomatic: Earthwork qi is yellow.

sentences.zh: 車氣乍高乍下，往往而聚。

translations.literal: Chariot-qi rises and falls abruptly, often clustering.
translations.idiomatic: Chariot-qi rises and falls abruptly, often clustering.

translations.literal: Cavalry qi is low and spread out.
translations.idiomatic: Cavalry qi is low and spread out.

translations.literal: Infantry qi is compact and clumped.
translations.idiomatic: Infantry qi is compact and clumped.

translations.literal: Low in front and high in rear means swift advance.
translations.idiomatic: Low in front and high in rear means swift advance.

translations.literal: Square in front and high behind means sharp striking force.
translations.idiomatic: Square in front and high behind means sharp striking force.

translations.literal: Sharp behind and low means retreat.
translations.idiomatic: Sharp behind and low means retreat.

translations.literal: If qi is level, movement is slow.
translations.idiomatic: If qi is level, movement is slow.

translations.literal: High in front and low behind means it will not stop and will turn back.
translations.idiomatic: High in front and low behind means it will not stop and will turn back.

sentences.zh: 氣相遇者，卑勝高，銳勝方。

translations.literal: When qi masses meet, low defeats high, sharp defeats square.
translations.idiomatic: When qi masses meet, low defeats high, sharp defeats square.

sentences.zh: 氣來卑而循車道者，不過三四日，去之五六里見。

translations.literal: If incoming qi is low and follows chariot roads, within three or four days it is seen five or six li away.
translations.idiomatic: If incoming qi is low and follows chariot roads, within three or four days it is seen five or six li away.

sentences.zh: 氣來高七八尺者，不過五六日，去之十餘二十里見。

translations.literal: If incoming qi is seven or eight chi high, within five or six days it is seen ten to twenty li away.
translations.idiomatic: If incoming qi is seven or eight chi high, within five or six days it is seen ten to twenty li away.

sentences.zh: 氣來高丈餘二丈者，不過三四十日，去之五六十里見。

translations.literal: If incoming qi is over one or two zhang high, within thirty or forty days it is seen fifty or sixty li away.
translations.idiomatic: If incoming qi is over one or two zhang high, within thirty or forty days it is seen fifty or sixty li away.

sentences.zh: 捎雲精白者，其將悍，其士怯。

translations.literal: If skimming clouds are bright white, the commander is fierce but troops are timid.
translations.idiomatic: If skimming clouds are bright white, the commander is fierce but troops are timid.

translations.literal: If cloud has broad root with far-cut front, there is battle.
translations.idiomatic: If cloud has broad root with far-cut front, there is battle.

translations.literal: If bright white and low-pointing rays, battle is won.
translations.idiomatic: If bright white and low-pointing rays, battle is won.

translations.literal: If front is red and raised, battle is not won.
translations.idiomatic: If front is red and raised, battle is not won.

translations.literal: Array-clouds are like standing walls.
translations.idiomatic: Array-clouds are like standing walls.

translations.literal: Shuttle-clouds resemble a shuttle.
translations.idiomatic: Shuttle-clouds resemble a shuttle.

translations.literal: Yuzu-clouds are compact with sharp tips.
translations.idiomatic: Yuzu-clouds are compact with sharp tips.

sentences.zh: 杓雲如繩者，居前竟天，其半半天。

translations.literal: Dipper-clouds, like ropes, stretch before the army across sky, sometimes half the sky.
translations.idiomatic: Dipper-clouds, like ropes, stretch before the army across sky, sometimes half the sky.

translations.literal: Rainbow clouds resemble battle flags.
translations.idiomatic: Rainbow clouds resemble battle flags.

translations.literal: Hooked-sharp clouds curve and bend.
translations.idiomatic: Hooked-sharp clouds curve and bend.

translations.literal: All these clouds are interpreted by five colors.
translations.idiomatic: All these clouds are interpreted by five colors.

sentences.zh: 而澤摶密，其見動人，乃有占；

translations.literal: If their luster is dense and compact, and their appearance stirs people, then there is an omen.
translations.idiomatic: If their luster is dense and compact, and their appearance stirs people, then there is an omen.

translations.literal: War will certainly rise.
translations.idiomatic: War will certainly rise.

translations.literal: In divining battle, read where it points.
translations.idiomatic: In divining battle, read where it points.

translations.literal: What Wang Shuo observed was judged by the sun's side.
translations.idiomatic: What Wang Shuo observed was judged by the sun's side.

translations.literal: Cloud qi beside the sun symbolizes the ruler.
translations.idiomatic: Cloud qi beside the sun symbolizes the ruler.

translations.literal: All are divined according to their forms.
translations.idiomatic: All are divined according to their forms.

sentences.zh: 故北夷之氣如群畜穹閭，南夷之氣類舟船幡旗。

translations.literal: Thus qi of northern barbarians is like herds and domed tents; qi of southern barbarians resembles boats, ships, and banners.
translations.idiomatic: Thus qi of northern barbarians is like herds and domed tents; qi of southern barbarians resembles boats, ships, and banners.

sentences.zh: 大水處，敗軍場，破國之虛，下有積泉，金寶上，皆有氣，不可不察。

translations.literal: At great-water sites, battlefields of defeated armies, empty lands of ruined states, places with underground springs, and places above buried treasure, all have qi and must be observed.
translations.idiomatic: At great-water sites, battlefields of defeated armies, empty lands of ruined states, places with underground springs, and places above buried treasure, all have qi and must be observed.

sentences.zh: 海旁蜃氣象樓臺，廣野氣成宮闕然。

translations.literal: By the sea, mirage-qi forms images of towers and terraces; over broad fields, qi forms palace-tower shapes.
translations.idiomatic: By the sea, mirage-qi forms images of towers and terraces; over broad fields, qi forms palace-tower shapes.

sentences.zh: 雲氣各象其山川人民所聚積。

translations.literal: Each cloud-qi takes the shape of its local mountains, rivers, and human concentrations.
translations.idiomatic: Each cloud-qi takes the shape of its local mountains, rivers, and human concentrations.

sentences.zh: 故侯息秏者，入國邑，視封疆田疇之整治，城郭室屋門戶之潤澤，次至車服畜產精華。

translations.literal: Therefore in judging waxing and waning, one enters towns and cities and observes order of borders and fields, moisture and vitality of walls, houses, and gates, then chariots, dress, livestock, and material brilliance.
translations.idiomatic: Therefore in judging waxing and waning, one enters towns and cities and observes order of borders and fields, moisture and vitality of walls, houses, and gates, then chariots, dress, livestock, and material brilliance.

translations.literal: Substantial thriving is auspicious; emptiness and depletion are inauspicious.
translations.idiomatic: Substantial thriving is auspicious; emptiness and depletion are inauspicious.

sentences.zh: 若煙非煙，若雲非雲，郁郁紛紛，蕭索輪囷，是謂慶雲。

translations.literal: If like smoke yet not smoke, like cloud yet not cloud, thickly clustered and tangled, sparse and rolling, this is called Auspicious Cloud.
translations.idiomatic: If like smoke yet not smoke, like cloud yet not cloud, thickly clustered and tangled, sparse and rolling, this is called Auspicious Cloud.

translations.literal: When Auspicious Cloud appears, it is joyful qi.
translations.idiomatic: When Auspicious Cloud appears, it is joyful qi.

sentences.zh: 若霧非霧，衣冠不濡，見則其城被甲而趨。

translations.literal: If like mist yet not mist, and clothes and caps are not dampened, then when it appears, that city's armored troops will rush out.
translations.idiomatic: If like mist yet not mist, and clothes and caps are not dampened, then when it appears, that city's armored troops will rush out.

sentences.zh: 夫雷電、赮鸶、辟歷、夜明者，陽氣之動者也，春夏則發，秋冬則藏，故候書者亡不司。

translations.literal: Thunder, lightning, sunset glows, rainbows, thunderbolts, and night-bright lights are movements of yang qi: they arise in spring and summer, are stored in autumn and winter; therefore omen manuals always account for them.
translations.idiomatic: Thunder, lightning, sunset glows, rainbows, thunderbolts, and night-bright lights are movements of yang qi: they arise in spring and summer, are stored in autumn and winter; therefore omen manuals always account for them.

translations.literal: Heaven opens and hangs forth objects; earth shakes and splits apart.
translations.idiomatic: Heaven opens and hangs forth objects; earth shakes and splits apart.

translations.literal: Mountains collapse and landslides occur; rivers are blocked and streams buried.
translations.idiomatic: Mountains collapse and landslides occur; rivers are blocked and streams buried.

translations.literal: Waters recede and land extends; marshes dry and forms are exposed.
translations.idiomatic: Waters recede and land extends; marshes dry and forms are exposed.

translations.literal: City walls, gates, and alleys are moist and thriving or dry and withered.
translations.idiomatic: City walls, gates, and alleys are moist and thriving or dry and withered.

translations.literal: Palaces, temples, corridors, and mansions are where people reside.
translations.idiomatic: Palaces, temples, corridors, and mansions are where people reside.

translations.literal: Songs, customs, chariots, and clothing reveal people's living habits.
translations.idiomatic: Songs, customs, chariots, and clothing reveal people's living habits.

translations.literal: Five grains and plants reveal what they are attached to.
translations.idiomatic: Five grains and plants reveal what they are attached to.

translations.literal: Granaries, depots, stables, armories, and roads in four directions.
translations.idiomatic: Granaries, depots, stables, armories, and roads in four directions.

translations.literal: Six livestock and wild beasts show where production goes and comes.
translations.idiomatic: Six livestock and wild beasts show where production goes and comes.

translations.literal: Fish, turtles, birds, and rats show where they dwell.
translations.idiomatic: Fish, turtles, birds, and rats show where they dwell.

translations.literal: Ghostly cries and howls, and chance encounters with uncanny beings.
translations.idiomatic: Ghostly cries and howls, and chance encounters with uncanny beings.

translations.literal: Rumors, whether true or not.
translations.idiomatic: Rumors, whether true or not.

translations.literal: In predicting year's good or bad, carefully observe at year's beginning.
translations.idiomatic: In predicting year's good or bad, carefully observe at year's beginning.

sentences.zh: 歲始或冬至日，產氣始萌。

translations.literal: Year's start may be winter solstice day, when generating qi first sprouts.
translations.idiomatic: Year's start may be winter solstice day, when generating qi first sprouts.

sentences.zh: 臘明日，人眾卒歲，壹會飲食，發陽氣，故曰初歲。

translations.literal: The day after La festival, people conclude old year and gather for common feast, releasing yang qi, therefore called year's beginning.
translations.idiomatic: The day after La festival, people conclude old year and gather for common feast, releasing yang qi, therefore called year's beginning.

translations.literal: First day of first month is ruler's year-start.
translations.idiomatic: First day of first month is ruler's year-start.

translations.literal: Beginning of spring is start of four seasons.
translations.idiomatic: Beginning of spring is start of four seasons.

translations.literal: These four beginnings are days for observation.
translations.idiomatic: These four beginnings are days for observation.

sentences.zh: 而漢魏鮮集臘明正月旦決八風。

translations.literal: In Han and Wei practice, they particularly gathered observations at La-next-day and first-month daybreak to determine the eight winds.
translations.idiomatic: In Han and Wei practice, they particularly gathered observations at La-next-day and first-month daybreak to determine the eight winds.

translations.literal: Wind from south: great drought.
translations.idiomatic: Wind from south: great drought.

translations.literal: From southwest: lesser drought.
translations.idiomatic: From southwest: lesser drought.

translations.literal: From west: warfare.
translations.idiomatic: From west: warfare.

translations.literal: From northwest: Rong and Shu disturbances, light rain, and rapid mobilization.
translations.idiomatic: From northwest: Rong and Shu disturbances, light rain, and rapid mobilization.

translations.literal: From north: average year.
translations.idiomatic: From north: average year.

translations.literal: From northeast: superior year.
translations.idiomatic: From northeast: superior year.

translations.literal: From east: great flooding.
translations.idiomatic: From east: great flooding.

translations.literal: From southeast: epidemics among people, and bad year.
translations.idiomatic: From southeast: epidemics among people, and bad year.

sentences.zh: 故八風各與其衝對，課多者為勝。

translations.literal: Thus each of eight winds is assessed against its opposite; the side occurring more often is stronger.
translations.idiomatic: Thus each of eight winds is assessed against its opposite; the side occurring more often is stronger.

translations.literal: More beats less, long-lasting beats sudden, rapid beats slow.
translations.idiomatic: More beats less, long-lasting beats sudden, rapid beats slow.

translations.literal: From dawn to breakfast-time corresponds to wheat.
translations.idiomatic: From dawn to breakfast-time corresponds to wheat.

translations.literal: From breakfast to sun decline corresponds to disease.
translations.idiomatic: From breakfast to sun decline corresponds to disease.

translations.literal: From decline to mid-afternoon corresponds to millet.
translations.idiomatic: From decline to mid-afternoon corresponds to millet.

translations.literal: From mid-afternoon to late afternoon corresponds to beans.
translations.idiomatic: From mid-afternoon to late afternoon corresponds to beans.

translations.literal: From late afternoon to sunset corresponds to hemp.
translations.idiomatic: From late afternoon to sunset corresponds to hemp.

sentences.zh: 欲終日有雲，有風，有日，當其時，深而多實；

translations.literal: If all day there are clouds, wind, and sun, and each appears in its proper time, crops root deep and bear much grain.
translations.idiomatic: If all day there are clouds, wind, and sun, and each appears in its proper time, crops root deep and bear much grain.

sentences.zh: 亡雲，有風日，當其時，淺而少實；

translations.literal: If no cloud but wind and sun at their proper times, roots are shallow and yields low.
translations.idiomatic: If no cloud but wind and sun at their proper times, roots are shallow and yields low.

sentences.zh: 有雲風，亡日，當其時，深而少實；

translations.literal: If cloud and wind but no sun at proper times, roots are deep but yields low.
translations.idiomatic: If cloud and wind but no sun at proper times, roots are deep but yields low.

sentences.zh: 有日，亡雲，不風，當其時者稼有敗。

translations.literal: If sun without cloud and no wind at proper times, crops suffer failure.
translations.idiomatic: If sun without cloud and no wind at proper times, crops suffer failure.

translations.literal: If only for one mealtime, minor failure.
translations.idiomatic: If only for one mealtime, minor failure.

translations.literal: If for as long as ripening five-dou rice, major failure.
translations.idiomatic: If for as long as ripening five-dou rice, major failure.

translations.literal: If wind rises again and clouds appear, crops recover.
translations.idiomatic: If wind rises again and clouds appear, crops recover.

sentences.zh: 各以其時用雲色占種所宜。

translations.literal: Use cloud colors at each time to divine which seeds are suitable.
translations.idiomatic: Use cloud colors at each time to divine which seeds are suitable.

translations.literal: Rain, snow, and cold mean a bad year.
translations.idiomatic: Rain, snow, and cold mean a bad year.

sentences.zh: 是日光明，聽都邑人民之聲。

translations.literal: On this day, with clear light, listen to sounds of city and populace.
translations.idiomatic: On this day, with clear light, listen to sounds of city and populace.

translations.literal: If in gong tone, year is good and auspicious.
translations.idiomatic: If in gong tone, year is good and auspicious.

translations.literal: If in shang, there is war.
translations.idiomatic: If in shang, there is war.

translations.literal: If in zhi, drought.
translations.idiomatic: If in zhi, drought.

translations.literal: If in yu, flood.
translations.idiomatic: If in yu, flood.

translations.literal: If in jue, year is bad.
translations.idiomatic: If in jue, year is bad.

translations.literal: Or from first day of first month onward count rainfall days.
translations.idiomatic: Or from first day of first month onward count rainfall days.

sentences.zh: 率日食一升，至七升而極；

translations.literal: At rate of one sheng per day, up to seven sheng is limit.
translations.idiomatic: At rate of one sheng per day, up to seven sheng is limit.

translations.literal: Beyond that, do not divine.
translations.idiomatic: Beyond that, do not divine.

sentences.zh: 數至十二日，直其月，占水旱。

translations.literal: Count to twelve days; align directly with that month to predict flood or drought.
translations.idiomatic: Count to twelve days; align directly with that month to predict flood or drought.

sentences.zh: 為其環域千里內占，即為天下候，竟正月。

translations.literal: Use this within a thousand-li circuit; if used for all under heaven, continue through full first month.
translations.idiomatic: Use this within a thousand-li circuit; if used for all under heaven, continue through full first month.

sentences.zh: 月所離列宿，日、風、雲，占其國。

translations.literal: For the lunar lodge where the moon departs, and for sun, wind, and cloud, divine that state.
translations.idiomatic: For the lunar lodge where the moon departs, and for sun, wind, and cloud, divine that state.

translations.literal: Yet one must certainly inspect where the Great Year is.
translations.idiomatic: Yet one must certainly inspect where the Great Year is.

translations.literal: If in metal, abundant harvest.
translations.idiomatic: If in metal, abundant harvest.

translations.literal: If in water, ruin.
translations.idiomatic: If in water, ruin.

translations.literal: If in wood, famine.
translations.idiomatic: If in wood, famine.

translations.literal: If in fire, drought.
translations.idiomatic: If in fire, drought.

translations.literal: This is the major canon.
translations.idiomatic: This is the major canon.

sentences.zh: 正月上甲，風從東方來，宜蠶；

translations.literal: On first jia day of first month, if wind comes from east, silkworm rearing is favorable.
translations.idiomatic: On first jia day of first month, if wind comes from east, silkworm rearing is favorable.

sentences.zh: 從西方來，若旦有黃雲，惡。

translations.literal: If it comes from west, and yellow cloud appears at dawn, it is inauspicious.
translations.idiomatic: If it comes from west, and yellow cloud appears at dawn, it is inauspicious.

sentences.zh: 冬至短極，縣土炭，炭動，麋鹿解角，蘭根出，泉水踊，略以知日至，要決晷景。

translations.literal: At winter solstice, when day is shortest, suspend earth and charcoal; when charcoal stirs, elk and deer shed antlers, orchid roots sprout, springs surge - these roughly indicate solstice, but final judgment relies on gnomon shadow.
translations.idiomatic: At winter solstice, when day is shortest, suspend earth and charcoal; when charcoal stirs, elk and deer shed antlers, orchid roots sprout, springs surge - these roughly indicate solstice, but final judgment relies on gnomon shadow.

sentences.zh: 夫天運三十歲一小變，百年中變，五百年大變，三大變一紀，三紀而大備，此其大數也。

translations.literal: Heaven's cycle has a minor change every 30 years, medium change every 100 years, major change every 500 years; three major changes make one epoch, three epochs complete the great cycle - this is its grand number.
translations.idiomatic: Heaven's cycle has a minor change every 30 years, medium change every 100 years, major change every 500 years; three major changes make one epoch, three epochs complete the great cycle - this is its grand number.

sentences.zh: 春秋二百四十二年間，日食三十六，彗星三見，夜常星不見，夜中星隕如雨者各一。

translations.literal: In the 242 years of the Spring and Autumn period, there were 36 solar eclipses, 3 comets, once when regular night stars disappeared, and once when stars fell like rain at night.
translations.idiomatic: In the 242 years of the Spring and Autumn period, there were 36 solar eclipses, 3 comets, once when regular night stars disappeared, and once when stars fell like rain at night.

sentences.zh: 當是時，禍亂輒應，周室微弱，上下交怨，殺君三十六，亡國五十二，諸侯奔走不得保其社稷者不可勝數。

translations.literal: At those times calamity and disorder always corresponded: Zhou house weakened, upper and lower resentful, 36 rulers killed, 52 states destroyed, and countless lords fled unable to preserve their altars.
translations.idiomatic: At those times calamity and disorder always corresponded: Zhou house weakened, upper and lower resentful, 36 rulers killed, 52 states destroyed, and countless lords fled unable to preserve their altars.

sentences.zh: 自是之後，眾暴寡，大并小。

translations.literal: After that, many oppressed few, great annexed small.
translations.idiomatic: After that, many oppressed few, great annexed small.

sentences.zh: 秦、楚、吳、粵，夷狄也，為彊伯。

translations.literal: Qin, Chu, Wu, and Yue, though once regarded as barbarians, became strong hegemons.
translations.idiomatic: Qin, Chu, Wu, and Yue, though once regarded as barbarians, became strong hegemons.

sentences.zh: 田氏篡齊，三家分晉，並為戰國，爭於攻取，兵革遞起，城邑數屠，因以飢饉疾疫愁苦，臣主共憂患，其察禨祥候星氣尤急。

translations.literal: The Tian clan usurped Qi, three houses divided Jin, making the Warring States; they contended in conquest, warfare rose in succession, cities repeatedly slaughtered, bringing famine, epidemic, and hardship; ruler and ministers shared anxieties, and observation of omens and star-qi became especially urgent.
translations.idiomatic: The Tian clan usurped Qi, three houses divided Jin, making the Warring States; they contended in conquest, warfare rose in succession, cities repeatedly slaughtered, bringing famine, epidemic, and hardship; ruler and ministers shared anxieties, and observation of omens and star-qi became especially urgent.

sentences.zh: 近世十二諸侯七國相王，言從橫者繼踵，而占天文者因時務論書傳，故其占驗鱗雜米鹽，亡可錄者。

translations.literal: In later times, twelve feudal houses and seven kings proclaimed themselves rulers; theorists of horizontal and vertical alliances followed one after another, and celestial diviners wrote according to current affairs, so their verified predictions became mixed like fish scales and grains of salt, with little worth recording.
translations.idiomatic: In later times, twelve feudal houses and seven kings proclaimed themselves rulers; theorists of horizontal and vertical alliances followed one after another, and celestial diviners wrote according to current affairs, so their verified predictions became mixed like fish scales and grains of salt, with little worth recording.

sentences.zh: 項羽救鉅鹿，枉矢西流，枉矢所觸，天下之所伐射，滅亡象也。

translations.literal: When Xiang Yu rescued Julu, Bent Arrow streamed westward; where Bent Arrow strikes is where the realm is attacked and shot, symbolizing destruction.
translations.idiomatic: When Xiang Yu rescued Julu, Bent Arrow streamed westward; where Bent Arrow strikes is where the realm is attacked and shot, symbolizing destruction.

sentences.zh: 物莫直於矢，今蛇行不能直而枉者，執矢者亦不正，以象項羽執政亂也。

translations.literal: Nothing is straighter than an arrow; now if it moves snakelike and bent, the one holding arrows is also not upright, symbolizing Xiang Yu's disorderly rule.
translations.idiomatic: Nothing is straighter than an arrow; now if it moves snakelike and bent, the one holding arrows is also not upright, symbolizing Xiang Yu's disorderly rule.

sentences.zh: 羽遂合從，阬秦人，屠咸陽。

translations.literal: Yu then formed a coalition, buried Qin troops alive, and massacred Xianyang.
translations.idiomatic: Yu then formed a coalition, buried Qin troops alive, and massacred Xianyang.

sentences.zh: 凡枉矢之流，以亂伐亂也。

translations.literal: In general, Bent Arrow's flow means using disorder to attack disorder.
translations.idiomatic: In general, Bent Arrow's flow means using disorder to attack disorder.

sentences.zh: 三年秋，太白出西方，有光幾中，乍北乍南，過期乃入。

translations.literal: In autumn of third year, Venus emerged in west, with light nearly central, shifting north and south, entering only after due period.
translations.idiomatic: In autumn of third year, Venus emerged in west, with light nearly central, shifting north and south, entering only after due period.

translations.literal: Mercury emerged at the four Meng positions.
translations.idiomatic: Mercury emerged at the four Meng positions.

sentences.zh: 是時，項羽為楚王，而漢已定三秦，與相距滎陽。

translations.literal: At that time Xiang Yu was king of Chu, while Han had already settled the Three Qins and faced him at Xingyang.
translations.idiomatic: At that time Xiang Yu was king of Chu, while Han had already settled the Three Qins and faced him at Xingyang.

sentences.zh: 太白出西方，有光幾中，是秦地戰將勝，而漢國將興也。

translations.literal: Venus emerging west with nearly central light meant warfare in Qin lands would be won and Han state would rise.
translations.idiomatic: Venus emerging west with nearly central light meant warfare in Qin lands would be won and Han state would rise.

sentences.zh: 辰星出四孟，易主之表也。

translations.literal: Mercury emerging at four Meng was a sign of changing rulership.
translations.idiomatic: Mercury emerging at four Meng was a sign of changing rulership.

translations.literal: Two years later, Han destroyed Chu.
translations.idiomatic: Two years later, Han destroyed Chu.

translations.literal: In year seven, lunar halo encircled Shen and Bi in seven layers.
translations.idiomatic: In year seven, lunar halo encircled Shen and Bi in seven layers.

translations.literal: The divination said: "Between Bi and Mao is Heavenly Street.
translations.idiomatic: The divination said: "Between Bi and Mao is Heavenly Street.

translations.literal: North of the street is Hu.
translations.idiomatic: North of the street is Hu.

translations.literal: South of the street is the central states.
translations.idiomatic: South of the street is the central states.

sentences.zh: 昴為匈奴，參為趙，畢為邊兵。」

translations.literal: Mao is Xiongnu, Shen is Zhao, Bi is frontier troops."
translations.idiomatic: Mao is Xiongnu, Shen is Zhao, Bi is frontier troops."

sentences.zh: 是歲高皇帝自將兵擊匈奴，至平城，為冒頓單于所圍，七日乃解。

translations.literal: That year Gao Emperor personally led troops against Xiongnu; at Pingcheng he was surrounded by Modun Chanyu and only escaped after seven days.
translations.idiomatic: That year Gao Emperor personally led troops against Xiongnu; at Pingcheng he was surrounded by Modun Chanyu and only escaped after seven days.

translations.literal: In twelfth year spring, Mars stationed at Heart.
translations.idiomatic: In twelfth year spring, Mars stationed at Heart.

translations.literal: In fourth month, the imperial carriage was mournfully stilled.
translations.idiomatic: In fourth month, the imperial carriage was mournfully stilled.

sentences.zh: 孝惠二年，天開東北，廣十餘丈，長二十餘丈。

translations.literal: In Emperor Hui year two, heaven opened in northeast, over ten zhang wide and over twenty zhang long.
translations.idiomatic: In Emperor Hui year two, heaven opened in northeast, over ten zhang wide and over twenty zhang long.

translations.literal: Earthquake means excess yin.
translations.idiomatic: Earthquake means excess yin.

sentences.zh: 天裂，陽不足：皆下盛彊將害上之變也。

translations.literal: Sky-rift means insufficient yang: both are signs that forces below are becoming too strong and will harm above.
translations.idiomatic: Sky-rift means insufficient yang: both are signs that forces below are becoming too strong and will harm above.

translations.literal: Afterward came the Lü clan disorder.
translations.idiomatic: Afterward came the Lü clan disorder.

sentences.zh: 孝文後二年正月壬寅，天欃夕出西南。

translations.literal: In Emperor Wen, later year two, first month renyin day, Heavenly Can appeared at dusk in southwest.
translations.idiomatic: In Emperor Wen, later year two, first month renyin day, Heavenly Can appeared at dusk in southwest.

translations.literal: Divination said: "It means warfare, mourning, and disorder."
translations.idiomatic: Divination said: "It means warfare, mourning, and disorder."

sentences.zh: 其六年十一月，匈奴入上郡、雲中，漢起三軍以衛京師。

translations.literal: In year six, eleventh month, Xiongnu entered Shang and Yunzhong commanderies; Han raised three armies to defend the capital.
